name: Astra DB

config_fields.name: embedding
config_fields.description: Embedding configuration

config_fields.name: indexing
config_fields.description: Astra DB gives developers the APIs, real-time data and ecosystem integrations to put accurate RAG and Gen AI apps with fewer hallucinations in production.

config_fields.name: omit_raw_text
config_fields.description: Do not store the text that gets embedded along with the vector and the metadata in the destination. If set to true, only the vector and the metadata will be stored - in this case raw text for LLM use cases needs to be retrieved from another source.

config_schema.description:
The configuration model for the Vector DB based destinations. This model is used to generate the UI for the destination configuration,
as well as to provide type safety for the configuration passed to the destination.

The configuration model is composed of four parts:
* Processing configuration
* Embedding configuration
* Indexing configuration
* Advanced configuration

Processing, embedding and advanced configuration are provided by this base class, while the indexing configuration is provided by the destination connector in the sub class.

oneOf.description: Use the OpenAI API to embed text. This option is using the text-embedding-ada-002 model with 1536 embedding dimensions.
oneOf.title: OpenAI

oneOf.description: Use the Cohere API to embed text.
oneOf.title: Cohere

oneOf.description: Use a fake embedding made out of random vectors with 1536 embedding dimensions. This is useful for testing the data pipeline without incurring any costs.
oneOf.title: Fake

oneOf.description: Use the Azure-hosted OpenAI API to embed text. This option is using the text-embedding-ada-002 model with 1536 embedding dimensions.

api_base.description: The base URL for your Azure OpenAI resource.  You can find this in the Azure portal under your Azure OpenAI resource
api_base.title: Resource base URL

deployment.description: The deployment for your Azure OpenAI resource.  You can find this in the Azure portal under your Azure OpenAI resource
deployment.title: Deployment

openai_key.description: The API key for your Azure OpenAI resource.  You can find this in the Azure portal under your Azure OpenAI resource
openai_key.title: Azure OpenAI API key

oneOf.title: Azure OpenAI

oneOf.description: Use a service that's compatible with the OpenAI API to embed text.

base_url.title: Base URL

dimensions.title: Embedding dimensions

model_name.title: Model name

indexing.description: Astra DB gives developers the APIs, real-time data and ecosystem integrations to put accurate RAG and Gen AI apps with fewer hallucinations in production.

astra_db_app_token.description: The application token authorizes a user to connect to a specific Astra DB database. It is created when the user clicks the Generate Token button on the Overview tab of the Database page in the Astra UI.
astra_db_app_token.title: Astra DB Application Token

astra_db_endpoint.description: The endpoint specifies which Astra DB database queries are sent to. It can be copied from the Database Details section of the Overview tab of the Database page in the Astra UI.
astra_db_endpoint.title: Astra DB Endpoint

astra_db_keyspace.description: Keyspaces (or Namespaces) serve as containers for organizing data within a database. You can create a new keyspace uisng the Data Explorer tab in the Astra UI. The keyspace default_keyspace is created for you when you create a Vector Database in Astra DB.
astra_db_keyspace.title: Astra DB Keyspace

collection.description: Collections hold data. They are analagous to tables in traditional Cassandra terminology. This tool will create the collection with the provided name automatically if it does not already exist. Alternatively, you can create one thorugh the Data Explorer tab in the Astra UI.
collection.title: Astra DB collection

indexing.title: Indexing

omit_raw_text.description: Do not store the text that gets embedded along with the vector and the metadata in the destination. If set to true, only the vector and the metadata will be stored - in this case raw text for LLM use cases needs to be retrieved from another source.
omit_raw_text.title: Do not store raw text

chunk_overlap.description: Size of overlap between chunks in tokens to store in vector store to better capture relevant context
chunk_overlap.title: Chunk overlap

chunk_size.description: Size of chunks in tokens to store in vector store (make sure it is not too big for the context if your LLM)
chunk_size.title: Chunk size

field_name_mappings.description: List of fields to rename. Not applicable for nested fields, but can be used to rename fields already flattened via dot notation.

from_field.description: The field name in the source
from_field.title: From field name

to_field.description: The field name to use in the destination
to_field.title: To field name

field_name_mappings.title: Field name mappings

metadata_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be stored as metadata. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered metadata fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array. When specifying nested paths, all matching values are flattened into an array set to a field named by the path.
metadata_fields.title: Fields to store as metadata

text_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be used to calculate the embedding. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered text fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array.
text_fields.title: Text fields to embed

text_splitter.description: Split text fields into chunks based on the specified method.

oneOf.description: Split the text by the list of separators until the chunk size is reached, using the earlier mentioned separators where possible. This is useful for splitting text fields by paragraphs, sentences, words, etc.

keep_separator.description: Whether to keep the separator in the resulting chunks
keep_separator.title: Keep separator

separators.description: List of separator strings to split text fields by. The separator itself needs to be wrapped in double quotes, e.g. to split by the dot character, use ".". To split by a newline, use "\n".
separators.title: Separators

oneOf.title: By Separator

oneOf.description: Split the text by Markdown headers down to the specified header level. If the chunk size fits multiple sections, they will be combined into a single chunk.

split_level.description: Level of markdown headers to split text fields by. Headings down to the specified level will be used as split points
split_level.title: Split level

oneOf.title: By Markdown header

oneOf.description: Split the text by suitable delimiters based on the programming language. This is useful for splitting code into chunks.

language.description: Split code in suitable places based on the programming language
language.title: Language

oneOf.title: By Programming Language

text_splitter.title: Text splitter

name: BigQuery

config_fields.name: cdc_deletion_mode
config_fields.description: Whether to execute CDC deletions as hard deletes (i.e. propagate source deletions to the destination), or soft deletes (i.e. leave a tombstone record in the destination). Defaults to hard deletes.

config_fields.name: credentials_json
config_fields.description: The contents of the JSON service account key. Check out the docs if you need help generating this key. Default credentials will be used if this field is left empty.

config_fields.name: dataset_id
config_fields.description: The default BigQuery Dataset ID that tables are replicated to if the source does not specify a namespace. Read more here.

config_fields.name: dataset_location
config_fields.description: The location of the dataset. Warning: Changes made after creation will not be applied. Read more here.

config_fields.name: disable_type_dedupe
config_fields.description: Write the legacy "raw tables" format, to enable backwards compatibility with older versions of this connector.

config_fields.name: loading_method
config_fields.description: The way data will be uploaded to BigQuery.

config_fields.name: project_id
config_fields.description: The GCP project ID for the project containing the target BigQuery dataset. Read more here.

config_fields.name: raw_data_dataset
config_fields.description: Airbyte will use this dataset for various internal tables. In legacy raw tables mode, the raw tables will be stored in this dataset. Defaults to "airbyte_internal".

cdc_deletion_mode.description: Whether to execute CDC deletions as hard deletes (i.e. propagate source deletions to the destination), or soft deletes (i.e. leave a tombstone record in the destination). Defaults to hard deletes.
cdc_deletion_mode.title: CDC deletion mode

credentials_json.description: The contents of the JSON service account key. Check out the docs if you need help generating this key. Default credentials will be used if this field is left empty.
credentials_json.title: Service Account Key JSON (Required for cloud, optional for open-source)

dataset_id.description: The default BigQuery Dataset ID that tables are replicated to if the source does not specify a namespace. Read more here.
dataset_id.title: Default Dataset ID

dataset_location.description: The location of the dataset. Warning: Changes made after creation will not be applied. Read more here.
dataset_location.title: Dataset Location

disable_type_dedupe.description: Write the legacy "raw tables" format, to enable backwards compatibility with older versions of this connector.
disable_type_dedupe.title: Legacy raw tables

loading_method.description: The way data will be uploaded to BigQuery.

oneOf.description: Direct loading using batched SQL INSERT statements. This method uses the BigQuery driver to convert large INSERT statements into file uploads automatically.
oneOf.title: Batched Standard Inserts

oneOf.description: Writes large batches of records to a file, uploads the file to GCS, then uses COPY INTO to load your data into BigQuery.

credential.description: An HMAC key is a type of credential and can be associated with a service account or a user account in Cloud Storage. Read more here.

hmac_key_access_id.description: HMAC key access ID. When linked to a service account, this ID is 61 characters long; when linked to a user account, it is 24 characters long.
hmac_key_access_id.title: HMAC Access Key

hmac_key_secret.description: The corresponding secret for the access ID. It is a 40-character base-64 encoded string.
hmac_key_secret.title: HMAC Secret

credential.title: Credential

gcs_bucket_name.description: The name of the GCS bucket. Read more here.
gcs_bucket_name.title: GCS Bucket Name

gcs_bucket_path.description: Directory under the GCS bucket where data will be written.
gcs_bucket_path.title: GCS Bucket Path

keep_files_in_gcs-bucket.default: Delete all tmp files from GCS
keep_files_in_gcs-bucket.description: This upload method is supposed to temporary store records in GCS bucket. By this select you can chose if these records should be removed from GCS when migration has finished. The default "Delete all tmp files from GCS" value is used if not set explicitly.
enum: Delete all tmp files from GCS
Keep all tmp files in GCS
keep_files_in_gcs-bucket.title: GCS Tmp Files Post-Processing

oneOf.title: GCS Staging

loading_method.title: Loading Method

project_id.description: The GCP project ID for the project containing the target BigQuery dataset. Read more here.
project_id.title: Project ID

raw_data_dataset.description: Airbyte will use this dataset for various internal tables. In legacy raw tables mode, the raw tables will be stored in this dataset. Defaults to "airbyte_internal".
raw_data_dataset.title: Airbyte Internal Table Dataset Name

format.description: Output data format. One of the following formats must be selected - AVRO format, PARQUET format, CSV format, or JSONL format.

compression_codec.description: The compression algorithm used to compress data. Default to no compression.

compression_level.description: 0: no compression & fastest, 9: best compression & slowest.
compression_level.title: Deflate level

compression_level.description: The presets 0-3 are fast presets with medium compression. The presets 4-6 are fairly slow presets with high compression. The default preset is 6. The presets 7-9 are like the preset 6 but use bigger dictionaries and have higher compressor and decompressor memory requirements. Unless the uncompressed size of the file exceeds 8 MiB, 16 MiB, or 32 MiB, it is waste of memory to use the presets 7, 8, or 9, respectively. Read more here for details.
compression_level.title: Compression Level

compression_level.description: Negative levels are 'fast' modes akin to lz4 or snappy, levels above 9 are generally for archival purposes, and levels above 18 use a lot of memory.
compression_level.title: Compression Level

include_checksum.description: If true, include a checksum with each data block.
include_checksum.title: Include Checksum

compression_codec.title: Compression Codec

compression.description: Whether the output files should be compressed. If compression is selected, the output filename will have an extra extension (GZIP: ".csv.gz").
compression.title: Compression

flattening.description: Whether the input JSON data should be normalized (flattened) in the output CSV. Please refer to docs for details.
flattening.title: Normalization

compression.description: Whether the output files should be compressed. If compression is selected, the output filename will have an extra extension (GZIP: ".jsonl.gz").
compression.title: Compression

block_size_mb.description: This is the size of a row group being buffered in memory. It limits the memory usage when writing. Larger values will improve the IO when reading, but consume more memory when writing. Default: 128 MB.
block_size_mb.title: Block Size (Row Group Size) (MB)

compression_codec.description: The compression algorithm used to compress data pages.
compression_codec.title: Compression Codec

dictionary_page_size_kb.description: There is one dictionary page per column per row group when dictionary encoding is used. The dictionary page size works like the page size but for dictionary. Default: 1024 KB.
dictionary_page_size_kb.title: Dictionary Page Size (KB)

max_padding_size_mb.description: Maximum size allowed as padding to align row groups. This is also the minimum size of a row group. Default: 8 MB.
max_padding_size_mb.title: Max Padding Size (MB)

page_size_kb.description: The page size is for compression. A block is composed of pages. A page is the smallest unit that must be read fully to access a single record. If this value is too small, the compression will deteriorate. Default: 1024 KB.
page_size_kb.title: Page Size (KB)

name: Kafka

config_fields.name: acks
config_fields.description: The number of acknowledgments the producer requires the leader to have received before considering a request complete. This controls the  durability of records that are sent.

config_fields.name: batch_size
config_fields.description: The producer will attempt to batch records together into fewer requests whenever multiple records are being sent to the same partition.

config_fields.name: bootstrap_servers
config_fields.description: A list of host/port pairs to use for establishing the initial connection to the Kafka cluster. The client will make use of all servers irrespective of which servers are specified here for bootstrapping—this list only impacts the initial hosts used to discover the full set of servers. This list should be in the form host1:port1,host2:port2,.... Since these servers are just used for the initial connection to discover the full cluster membership (which may change dynamically), this list need not contain the full set of servers (you may want more than one, though, in case a server is down).

config_fields.name: buffer_memory
config_fields.description: The total bytes of memory the producer can use to buffer records waiting to be sent to the server.

config_fields.name: client_dns_lookup
config_fields.description: Controls how the client uses DNS lookups. If set to use_all_dns_ips, connect to each returned IP address in sequence until a successful connection is established. After a disconnection, the next IP is used. Once all IPs have been used once, the client resolves the IP(s) from the hostname again. If set to resolve_canonical_bootstrap_servers_only, resolve each bootstrap address into a list of canonical names. After the bootstrap phase, this behaves the same as use_all_dns_ips. If set to default (deprecated), attempt to connect to the first IP address returned by the lookup, even if the lookup returns multiple IP addresses.

config_fields.name: client_id
config_fields.description: An ID string to pass to the server when making requests. The purpose of this is to be able to track the source of requests beyond just ip/port by allowing a logical application name to be included in server-side request logging.

config_fields.name: compression_type
config_fields.description: The compression type for all data generated by the producer.

config_fields.name: delivery_timeout_ms
config_fields.description: An upper bound on the time to report success or failure after a call to 'send()' returns.

config_fields.name: enable_idempotence
config_fields.description: When set to 'true', the producer will ensure that exactly one copy of each message is written in the stream. If 'false', producer retries due to broker failures, etc., may write duplicates of the retried message in the stream.

config_fields.name: linger_ms
config_fields.description: The producer groups together any records that arrive in between request transmissions into a single batched request.

config_fields.name: max_block_ms
config_fields.description: The configuration controls how long the KafkaProducer's send(), partitionsFor(), initTransactions(), sendOffsetsToTransaction(), commitTransaction() and abortTransaction() methods will block.

config_fields.name: max_in_flight_requests_per_connection
config_fields.description: The maximum number of unacknowledged requests the client will send on a single connection before blocking. Can be greater than 1, and the maximum value supported with idempotency is 5.

config_fields.name: max_request_size
config_fields.description: The maximum size of a request in bytes.

config_fields.name: protocol
config_fields.description: Protocol used to communicate with brokers.

config_fields.name: receive_buffer_bytes
config_fields.description: The size of the TCP receive buffer (SO_RCVBUF) to use when reading data. If the value is -1, the OS default will be used.

config_fields.name: request_timeout_ms
config_fields.description: The configuration controls the maximum amount of time the client will wait for the response of a request. If the response is not received before the timeout elapses the client will resend the request if necessary or fail the request if retries are exhausted.

config_fields.name: retries
config_fields.description: Setting a value greater than zero will cause the client to resend any record whose send fails with a potentially transient error.

config_fields.name: send_buffer_bytes
config_fields.description: The size of the TCP send buffer (SO_SNDBUF) to use when sending data. If the value is -1, the OS default will be used.

config_fields.name: socket_connection_setup_timeout_max_ms
config_fields.description: The maximum amount of time the client will wait for the socket connection to be established. The connection setup timeout will increase exponentially for each consecutive connection failure up to this maximum.

config_fields.name: socket_connection_setup_timeout_ms
config_fields.description: The amount of time the client will wait for the socket connection to be established.

config_fields.name: sync_producer
config_fields.description: Wait synchronously until the record has been sent to Kafka.

config_fields.name: test_topic
config_fields.description: Topic to test if Airbyte can produce messages.

config_fields.name: topic_pattern
config_fields.description: Topic pattern in which the records will be sent. You can use patterns like '{namespace}' and/or '{stream}' to send the message to a specific topic based on these values. Notice that the topic name will be transformed to a standard naming convention.

acks.description: The number of acknowledgments the producer requires the leader to have received before considering a request complete. This controls the  durability of records that are sent.

config_fields.name: omit_raw_text
config_fields.description: Do not store the text that gets embedded along with the vector and the metadata in the destination. If set to true, only the vector and the metadata will be stored - in this case raw text for LLM use cases needs to be retrieved from another source.

config_schema.description:
The configuration model for the Vector DB based destinations. This model is used to generate the UI for the destination configuration,
as well as to provide type safety for the configuration passed to the destination.

The configuration model is composed of four parts:
* Processing configuration
* Embedding configuration
* Indexing configuration
* Advanced configuration

Processing, embedding and advanced configuration are provided by this base class, while the indexing configuration is provided by the destination connector in the sub class.

vector_field.title: Vector Field

omit_raw_text.title: Do not store raw text

chunk_overlap.title: Chunk overlap

chunk_size.title: Chunk size

field_name_mappings.description: List of fields to rename. Not applicable for nested fields, but can be used to rename fields already flattened via dot notation.

from_field.description: The field name in the source
from_field.title: From field name

to_field.description: The field name to use in the destination
to_field.title: To field name

field_name_mappings.title: Field name mappings

metadata_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be stored as metadata. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered metadata fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array. When specifying nested paths, all matching values are flattened into an array set to a field named by the path.
metadata_fields.title: Fields to store as metadata

text_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be used to calculate the embedding. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered text fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array.
text_fields.title: Text fields to embed

text_splitter.description: Split text fields into chunks based on the specified method.

oneOf.description: Split the text by the list of separators until the chunk size is reached, using the earlier mentioned separators where possible. This is useful for splitting text fields by paragraphs, sentences, words, etc.

keep_separator.description: Whether to keep the separator in the resulting chunks
keep_separator.title: Keep separator

separators.description: List of separator strings to split text fields by. The separator itself needs to be wrapped in double quotes, e.g. to split by the dot character, use ".". To split by a newline, use "\n".
separators.title: Separators

oneOf.title: By Separator

oneOf.description: Split the text by Markdown headers down to the specified header level. If the chunk size fits multiple sections, they will be combined into a single chunk.

split_level.description: Level of markdown headers to split text fields by. Headings down to the specified level will be used as split points
split_level.title: Split level

oneOf.title: By Markdown header

oneOf.description: Split the text by suitable delimiters based on the programming language. This is useful for splitting code into chunks.

language.description: Split code in suitable places based on the programming language
language.title: Language

oneOf.title: By Programming Language

text_splitter.title: Text splitter

name: PGVector

config_fields.name: embedding
config_fields.description: Embedding configuration

config_fields.name: indexing
config_fields.description: Postgres can be used to store vector data and retrieve embeddings.

config_fields.name: omit_raw_text
config_fields.description: Do not store the text that gets embedded along with the vector and the metadata in the destination. If set to true, only the vector and the metadata will be stored - in this case raw text for LLM use cases needs to be retrieved from another source.

config_schema.description:
The configuration model for the Vector DB based destinations. This model is used to generate the UI for the destination configuration,
as well as to provide type safety for the configuration passed to the destination.

The configuration model is composed of four parts:
* Processing configuration
* Embedding configuration
* Indexing configuration
* Advanced configuration

Processing, embedding and advanced configuration are provided by this base class, while the indexing configuration is provided by the destination connector in the sub class.

oneOf.description: Use the OpenAI API to embed text. This option is using the text-embedding-ada-002 model with 1536 embedding dimensions.
oneOf.title: OpenAI

oneOf.description: Use the Cohere API to embed text.
oneOf.title: Cohere

oneOf.description: Use a fake embedding made out of random vectors with 1536 embedding dimensions. This is useful for testing the data pipeline without incurring any costs.
oneOf.title: Fake

oneOf.description: Use the Azure-hosted OpenAI API to embed text. This option is using the text-embedding-ada-002 model with 1536 embedding dimensions.

api_base.title: Resource base URL

deployment.title: Deployment

openai_key.title: Azure OpenAI API key

oneOf.title: Azure OpenAI

base_url.title: Base URL

dimensions.title: Embedding dimensions

model_name.description: The name of the model to use for embedding
model_name.title: Model name

password.title: Password

host.title: Host

omit_raw_text.description: Do not store the text that gets embedded along with the vector and the metadata in the destination. If set to true, only the vector and the metadata will be stored - in this case raw text for LLM use cases needs to be retrieved from another source.
omit_raw_text.title: Do not store raw text

chunk_overlap.description: Size of overlap between chunks in tokens to store in vector store to better capture relevant context
chunk_overlap.title: Chunk overlap

chunk_size.description: Size of chunks in tokens to store in vector store (make sure it is not too big for the context if your LLM)
chunk_size.title: Chunk size

field_name_mappings.description: List of fields to rename. Not applicable for nested fields, but can be used to rename fields already flattened via dot notation.

from_field.description: The field name in the source
from_field.title: From field name

to_field.description: The field name to use in the destination
to_field.title: To field name

field_name_mappings.title: Field name mappings

metadata_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be stored as metadata. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered metadata fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array. When specifying nested paths, all matching values are flattened into an array set to a field named by the path.
metadata_fields.title: Fields to store as metadata

text_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be used to calculate the embedding. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered text fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array.
text_fields.title: Text fields to embed

text_splitter.description: Split text fields into chunks based on the specified method.

oneOf.description: Split the text by the list of separators until the chunk size is reached, using the earlier mentioned separators where possible. This is useful for splitting text fields by paragraphs, sentences, words, etc.

keep_separator.description: Whether to keep the separator in the resulting chunks
keep_separator.title: Keep separator

separators.description: List of separator strings to split text fields by. The separator itself needs to be wrapped in double quotes, e.g. to split by the dot character, use ".". To split by a newline, use "\n".
separators.title: Separators

oneOf.title: By Separator

oneOf.description: Split the text by Markdown headers down to the specified header level. If the chunk size fits multiple sections, they will be combined into a single chunk.

split_level.description: Level of markdown headers to split text fields by. Headings down to the specified level will be used as split points
split_level.title: Split level

oneOf.title: By Markdown header

oneOf.description: Split the text by suitable delimiters based on the programming language. This is useful for splitting code into chunks.

language.description: Split code in suitable places based on the programming language
language.title: Language

oneOf.title: By Programming Language

text_splitter.title: Text splitter

name: Postgres

config_fields.name: cdc_deletion_mode
config_fields.description: Whether to execute CDC deletions as hard deletes (i.e. propagate source deletions to the destination), or soft deletes (i.e. leave a tombstone record in the destination). Defaults to hard deletes.

config_fields.name: database
config_fields.description: Name of the database.

config_fields.name: disable_type_dedupe
config_fields.description: Disable Writing Final Tables. WARNING! The data format in _airbyte_data is likely stable but there are no guarantees that other metadata columns will remain the same in future versions

config_fields.name: drop_cascade
config_fields.description: Drop tables with CASCADE. WARNING! This will delete all data in all dependent objects (views, etc.). Use with caution. This option is intended for usecases which can easily rebuild the dependent objects.

config_fields.name: host
config_fields.description: Hostname of the database.

config_fields.name: jdbc_url_params
config_fields.description: Additional properties to pass to the JDBC URL string when connecting to the database formatted as 'key=value' pairs separated by the symbol '&'. (example: key1=value1&key2=value2&key3=value3).

config_fields.name: password
config_fields.description: Password associated with the username.

config_fields.name: port
config_fields.description: Port of the database.

config_fields.name: raw_data_schema
config_fields.description: Airbyte will use this schema for various internal tables. In legacy raw tables mode, the raw tables will be stored in this schema. Defaults to "airbyte_internal".

config_fields.name: schema
config_fields.description: The default schema tables are written. If not specified otherwise, the "public" schema will be used.

config_fields.name: ssl
config_fields.description: Encrypt data using SSL. When activating SSL, please select one of the connection modes.

config_fields.name: ssl_mode
config_fields.description: SSL connection modes. disable - Disables encryption of communication between Airbyte and destination database. allow - Enables encryption only when required by the destination database. prefer - Allows unencrypted connections only if the destination database does not support encryption. require - Always require encryption. If the destination database server does not support encryption, connection will fail. verify-ca - Always require encryption and verifies that the destination database server has a valid SSL certificate. verify-full - This is the most secure mode. Always require encryption and verifies the identity of the destination database server. See more information - in the docs.

config_schema.description:
The configuration model for the Vector DB based destinations. This model is used to generate the UI for the destination configuration,
as well as to provide type safety for the configuration passed to the destination.

The configuration model is composed of four parts:
* Processing configuration
* Embedding configuration
* Indexing configuration
* Advanced configuration

Processing, embedding and advanced configuration are provided by this base class, while the indexing configuration is provided by the destination connector in the sub class.

to_field.description: The field name to use in the destination

field_name_mappings.title: Field name mappings

metadata_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be stored as metadata. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered metadata fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array. When specifying nested paths, all matching values are flattened into an array set to a field named by the path.
metadata_fields.title: Fields to store as metadata

text_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be used to calculate the embedding. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered text fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array.
text_fields.title: Text fields to embed

text_splitter.description: Split text fields into chunks based on the specified method.

oneOf.description: Split the text by the list of separators until the chunk size is reached, using the earlier mentioned separators where possible. This is useful for splitting text fields by paragraphs, sentences, words, etc.

keep_separator.description: Whether to keep the separator in the resulting chunks
keep_separator.title: Keep separator

separators.description: List of separator strings to split text fields by. The separator itself needs to be wrapped in double quotes, e.g. to split by the dot character, use ".". To split by a newline, use "\n".
separators.title: Separators

oneOf.title: By Separator

oneOf.description: Split the text by Markdown headers down to the specified header level. If the chunk size fits multiple sections, they will be combined into a single chunk.

split_level.description: Level of markdown headers to split text fields by. Headings down to the specified level will be used as split points
split_level.title: Split level

oneOf.title: By Markdown header

oneOf.description: Split the text by suitable delimiters based on the programming language. This is useful for splitting code into chunks.

language.description: Split code in suitable places based on the programming language
language.title: Language

oneOf.title: By Programming Language

text_splitter.title: Text splitter

max_padding_size_mb.description: Maximum size allowed as padding to align row groups. This is also the minimum size of a row group. Default: 8 MB.

page_size_kb.description: The page size is for compression. A block is composed of pages. A page is the smallest unit that must be read fully to access a single record. If this value is too small, the compression will deteriorate. Default: 1024 KB.

config_fields.description: Embedding configuration

config_fields.description: Snowflake can be used to store vector data and retrieve embeddings.

config_fields.name: omit_raw_text
config_fields.description: Do not store the text that gets embedded along with the vector and the metadata in the destination. If set to true, only the vector and the metadata will be stored - in this case raw text for LLM use cases needs to be retrieved from another source.

config_schema.description:
The configuration model for the Vector DB based destinations. This model is used to generate the UI for the destination configuration,
as well as to provide type safety for the configuration passed to the destination.

The configuration model is composed of four parts:
* Processing configuration
* Embedding configuration
* Indexing configuration
* Advanced configuration

Processing, embedding and advanced configuration are provided by this base class, while the indexing configuration is provided by the destination connector in the sub class.

chunk_size.title: Chunk size

field_name_mappings.description: List of fields to rename. Not applicable for nested fields, but can be used to rename fields already flattened via dot notation.

from_field.description: The field name in the source
from_field.title: From field name

to_field.description: The field name to use in the destination
to_field.title: To field name

field_name_mappings.title: Field name mappings

metadata_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be stored as metadata. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered metadata fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array. When specifying nested paths, all matching values are flattened into an array set to a field named by the path.
metadata_fields.title: Fields to store as metadata

text_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be used to calculate the embedding. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered text fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array.
text_fields.title: Text fields to embed

text_splitter.description: Split text fields into chunks based on the specified method.

oneOf.description: Split the text by the list of separators until the chunk size is reached, using the earlier mentioned separators where possible. This is useful for splitting text fields by paragraphs, sentences, words, etc.

keep_separator.description: Whether to keep the separator in the resulting chunks
keep_separator.title: Keep separator

separators.description: List of separator strings to split text fields by. The separator itself needs to be wrapped in double quotes, e.g. to split by the dot character, use ".". To split by a newline, use "\n".
separators.title: Separators

oneOf.title: By Separator

oneOf.description: Split the text by Markdown headers down to the specified header level. If the chunk size fits multiple sections, they will be combined into a single chunk.

split_level.description: Level of markdown headers to split text fields by. Headings down to the specified level will be used as split points
split_level.title: Split level

oneOf.title: By Markdown header

oneOf.description: Split the text by suitable delimiters based on the programming language. This is useful for splitting code into chunks.

language.description: Split code in suitable places based on the programming language
language.title: Language

oneOf.title: By Programming Language

text_splitter.title: Text splitter

name: Vectara

config_fields.name: corpus_name
config_fields.description: The Name of Corpus to load data into

config_fields.name: customer_id
config_fields.description: Your customer id as it is in the authenticaion url

config_fields.name: metadata_fields
config_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be stored as metadata. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered metadata fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array. When specifying nested paths, all matching values are flattened into an array set to a field named by the path.

config_fields.name: oauth2
config_fields.description: OAuth2.0 credentials used to authenticate admin actions (creating/deleting corpora)

config_fields.name: parallelize
config_fields.description: Parallelize indexing into Vectara with multiple threads

config_fields.name: text_fields
config_fields.description: List of fields in the record that should be in the section of the document. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered text fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array.

config_fields.name: title_field
config_fields.description: A field that will be used to populate the `title` of each document. The field list is applied to all streams in the same way and non-existing fields are ignored. If none are defined, all fields are considered text fields. When specifying text fields, you can access nested fields in the record by using dot notation, e.g. `user.name` will access the `name` field in the `user` object. It's also possible to use wildcards to access all fields in an object, e.g. `users.*.name` will access all `names` fields in all entries of the `users` array.

config_fields.name: omit_raw_text

config_schema.description:
The configuration model for the Vector DB based destinations. This model is used to generate the UI for the destination configuration,
as well as to provide type safety for the configuration passed to the destination.

The configuration model is composed of four parts:
* Processing configuration
* Embedding configuration
* Indexing configuration
* Advanced configuration

Processing, embedding and advanced configuration are provided by this base class, while the indexing configuration is provided by the destination connector in the sub class.

name: Amazon Seller Partner

config_fields.name: account_type
config_fields.description: Type of the Account you're going to authorize the Airbyte application by

config_fields.name: app_id
config_fields.description: Your Amazon Application ID.

config_fields.name: aws_environment
config_fields.description: Select the AWS Environment.

config_fields.name: creation_requester_429_max_retries
config_fields.description: Maximum number of retry attempts when the report creation API returns HTTP 429 (Too Many Requests). Each retry uses exponential backoff based on the x-amzn-RateLimit-Limit header. Reduce this value to avoid exhausting rate limits on retrying requests.

config_fields.name: failed_retry_wait_time_in_seconds
config_fields.description: Time in seconds to wait before retrying a report that returned FATAL status. Amazon enforces per-report-type cooldowns after generating a report. Near-real-time FBA reports have an approximately 30-minute cooldown, daily FBA reports approximately 4 hours. The default of 1800 seconds covers the most common cooldown. Increase this value if you see repeated FATAL errors on daily FBA reports.

config_fields.name: financial_events_max_results_per_page
config_fields.description: The maximum number of results to return per page for the ListFinancialEvents stream. If the response exceeds the maximum number of transactions or 10 MB, the API returns an InvalidInput error. Lower this value if you encounter InvalidInput errors during sync. Valid range is 1-100.

config_fields.name: financial_events_step
config_fields.description:
The time window size for fetching financial events data in chunks for the ListFinancialEvents and ListFinancialEventGroups streams. Options include hourly (1H, 6H, 12H) and daily (1D, 7D, 14D, 30D, 60D, 90D, 180D) granularity.

- **Hourly step sizes (e.g., 1H, 6H)** are recommended for very high data volumes where daily windows cause pagination token expiration (TTL errors). They fetch smaller chunks per request, reducing the risk of timeouts.
- **Daily step sizes (e.g., 1D, 7D)** are better for moderate data volumes. They balance sync speed with API efficiency.
- **Larger step sizes (e.g., 30D, 180D)** are better for smaller data volumes. They fetch more data per request, speeding up syncing and reducing the number of API calls.

Select a step size that matches your data volume to optimize syncing speed and API performance.

config_fields.name: include_pii
config_fields.description: When enabled, the connector requests a Restricted Data Token (RDT) to access PII fields such as BuyerInfo and ShippingAddress in the Orders and OrderItems streams. Your Amazon SP-API developer profile must have an approved Restricted Role (Direct-to-Consumer Shipping or Tax Invoicing). If the RDT request is denied (HTTP 403), the connector falls back to the standard token automatically and PII fields remain empty.

config_fields.name: lwa_app_id
config_fields.description: Your Login with Amazon Client ID.

config_fields.name: lwa_client_secret
config_fields.description: Your Login with Amazon Client Secret.

config_fields.name: max_async_job_count
config_fields.description: The maximum number of concurrent asynchronous job requests that can be active at a time.

config_fields.name: max_done_report_age_hours
config_fields.description: When the connector finds an existing completed (DONE) report matching the same date range and marketplace, it can reuse that report instead of creating a new one. This setting controls how old (in hours) a completed report can be and still be reused. Set to 0 to always create new reports. Maximum is 72 hours.

config_fields.name: num_workers
config_fields.description: The number of workers to use for the connector when syncing concurrently.

config_fields.name: period_in_days
config_fields.description: For syncs spanning a large date range, this option is used to request data in a smaller fixed window to improve sync reliability. This time window can be configured granularly by day.

config_fields.name: refresh_token
config_fields.description: The Refresh Token obtained via OAuth flow authorization.

config_fields.name: region
config_fields.description: Select the AWS Region.

config_fields.name: replication_end_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00Z. Any data after this date will not be replicated.

config_fields.name: replication_start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00Z. Any data before this date will not be replicated. If start date is not provided or older than 2 years ago from today, the date 2 years ago from today will be used.

config_fields.name: report_options_list
config_fields.description: Additional information passed to reports. This varies by report type.

config_fields.name: report_stream_lookback_window_in_hours
config_fields.description: Number of hours to re-fetch for incremental report streams on each sync. Increase this value if Amazon continues updating report data after the previous sync has completed. Duplicates from the lookback window are handled by destination deduplication. Set to 0 to disable. This does not apply to GET_SALES_AND_TRAFFIC_REPORT_BY_MONTH or GET_VENDOR_SALES_REPORT, which use monthly or date-only report boundaries.

config_fields.name: sales_and_traffic_report_asin_granularity
config_fields.description: The level of ASIN granularity for the Sales and Traffic report streams. PARENT returns data aggregated at the parent ASIN level. CHILD returns data at the child ASIN level with populated childAsin values. SKU returns data at the individual SKU level with populated childAsin and sku values.

config_fields.name: stop_sync_on_rate_limit
config_fields.description: Only applies to report streams. When enabled, the source stops retrying immediately once the rate limit retry budget is exhausted and fails with an actionable configuration error. This is useful when persistent rate limiting indicates the connector configuration needs adjustment (such as reducing the number of report streams or increasing the sync interval). When disabled (default), the source applies its backoff and retry strategy; if all retry attempts are exhausted, a transient error is thrown and the sync may be rescheduled automatically.

config_fields.name: wait_to_avoid_fatal_errors
config_fields.description: Deprecated - this option is no longer functional and will be removed in a future version. Rate limiting is now handled automatically by the connector.

oneOf.description: Recommended - Extract and load structured records into your destination of choice. This is the classic method of moving data in Airbyte. It allows for blocking and hashing individual fields or files from a structured schema. Data can be flattened, typed and deduped depending on the destination.
oneOf.title: Replicate Records

oneOf.description: Copy raw files without parsing their contents. Bits are copied into the destination exactly as they appeared in the source. Recommended for use with unstructured text data, non-text and compressed files.

preserve_directory_structure.description: If enabled, sends subdirectory folder structure along with source file names to the destination. Otherwise, files will be synced by their names only. This option is ignored when file-based replication is not enabled.
preserve_directory_structure.title: Preserve Sub-Directories in File Paths

oneOf.title: Copy Raw Files

start_date.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00.000000Z. Any file modified before this date will not be replicated.
start_date.title: Start Date

streams.description: Each instance of this configuration defines a stream. Use this to define which files belong in the stream, their format, and how they should be parsed and validated. When sending data to warehouse destination such as Snowflake or BigQuery, each stream is a separate table.

days_to_sync_if_history_is_full.description: When the state history of the file store is full, syncs will only read files that were last modified in the provided day range.
days_to_sync_if_history_is_full.title: Days To Sync If History Is Full

format.description: The configuration options that are used to alter how to read incoming files that deviate from the standard formatting.

double_as_string.description: Whether to convert double fields to strings. This is recommended if you have decimal numbers with a high degree of precision because there can be a loss precision when handling floating point numbers.
double_as_string.title: Convert Double Fields to Strings

delimiter.description: The character delimiting individual cells in the CSV data. This may only be a 1-character string. For tab-delimited data enter '\t'.
delimiter.title: Delimiter

double_quote.description: Whether two quotes in a quoted CSV value denote a single quote in the data.
double_quote.title: Double Quote

encoding.description: The character encoding of the CSV data. Leave blank to default to UTF8. See list of python encodings for allowable options.
encoding.title: Encoding

escape_char.description: The character used for escaping special characters. To disallow escaping, leave this field blank.
escape_char.title: Escape Character

false_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as false values.
false_values.title: False Values

header_definition.description: How headers will be defined. `User Provided` assumes the CSV does not have a header row and uses the headers provided and `Autogenerated` assumes the CSV does not have a header row and the CDK will generate headers using for `f{i}` where `i` is the index starting from 0. Else, the default behavior is to use the header from the CSV file. If a user wants to autogenerate or provide column names for a CSV having headers, they can skip rows.

column_names.description: The column names that will be used while emitting the CSV records
column_names.title: Column Names

header_definition.title: CSV Header Definition

ignore_errors_on_fields_mismatch.description: Whether to ignore errors that occur when the number of fields in the CSV does not match the number of columns in the schema.
ignore_errors_on_fields_mismatch.title: Ignore errors on field mismatch

inference_type.description: How to infer the types of the columns. If none, inference default to strings.
inference_type.title: Inference Type

null_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as null values. For example, if the value 'NA' should be interpreted as null, enter 'NA' in this field.
null_values.title: Null Values

quote_char.description: The character used for quoting CSV values. To disallow quoting, make this field blank.
quote_char.title: Quote Character

skip_rows_after_header.description: The number of rows to skip after the header row.
skip_rows_after_header.title: Skip Rows After Header

skip_rows_before_header.description: The number of rows to skip before the header row. For example, if the header row is on the 3rd row, enter 2 in this field.
skip_rows_before_header.title: Skip Rows Before Header

strings_can_be_null.description: Whether strings can be interpreted as null values. If true, strings that match the null_values set will be interpreted as null. If false, strings that match the null_values set will be interpreted as the string itself.
strings_can_be_null.title: Strings Can Be Null

true_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as true values.
true_values.title: True Values

decimal_as_float.description: Whether to convert decimal fields to floats. There is a loss of precision when converting decimals to floats, so this is not recommended.
decimal_as_float.title: Convert Decimal Fields to Floats

oneOf.description: Extract text from document formats (.pdf, .docx, .md, .pptx) and emit as one record per file.
Process files locally, supporting `fast` and `ocr` modes. This is the default option.
oneOf.title: Local

skip_unprocessable_files.description: If true, skip files that cannot be parsed and pass the error message along as the _ab_source_file_parse_error field. If false, fail the sync.
skip_unprocessable_files.title: Skip Unprocessable Files

strategy.description: The strategy used to parse documents. `fast` extracts text directly from the document which doesn't work for all files. `ocr_only` is more reliable, but slower. `hi_res` is the most reliable, but requires an API key and a hosted instance of unstructured and can't be used with local mode. See the unstructured.io documentation for more details: https://unstructured-io.github.io/unstructured/core/partition.html#partition-pdf
strategy.title: Parsing Strategy

oneOf.title: Unstructured Document Format

format.title: Format

globs.description: The pattern used to specify which files should be selected from the file system. For more information on glob pattern matching look here.
globs.title: Globs

input_schema.description: The schema that will be used to validate records extracted from the file. This will override the stream schema that is auto-detected from incoming files.
input_schema.title: Input Schema

legacy_prefix.description: The path prefix configured in v3 versions of the S3 connector. This option is deprecated in favor of a single glob.
legacy_prefix.title: Legacy Prefix

primary_key.description: The column or columns (for a composite key) that serves as the unique identifier of a record. If empty, the primary key will default to the parser's default primary key.
primary_key.title: Primary Key

recent_n_files_to_read_for_schema_discov.description: The number of resent files which will be used to discover the schema for this stream.
recent_n_files_to_read_for_schema_discov.title: Files To Read For Schema Discover

schemaless.description: When enabled, syncs will not validate or structure records against the stream's schema.
schemaless.title: Schemaless

use_first_found_file_for_schema_discover.description: When enabled, the source will use the first found file for schema discovery. Helps to avoid long discovery step.
use_first_found_file_for_schema_discover.title: Use First Found File For Schema Discover

validation_policy.description: The name of the validation policy that dictates sync behavior when a record does not adhere to the stream schema.

name: Facebook Marketing

config_fields.name: access_token
config_fields.description: The value of the generated access token. From your App’s Dashboard, click on "Marketing API" then "Tools". Select permissions ads_management, ads_read, read_insights, business_management. Then click on "Get token". See the docs for more information.

config_fields.name: account_ids
config_fields.description: The Facebook Ad account ID(s) to pull data from. The Ad account ID number is in the account dropdown menu or in your browser's address bar of your Meta Ads Manager. See the docs for more information.

config_fields.name: action_breakdowns_allow_empty
config_fields.description: Allows action_breakdowns to be an empty list

config_fields.name: ad_statuses
config_fields.description: Select the statuses you want to be loaded in the stream. If no specific statuses are selected, the API's default behavior applies, and some statuses may be filtered out.

config_fields.name: adset_statuses
config_fields.description: Select the statuses you want to be loaded in the stream. If no specific statuses are selected, the API's default behavior applies, and some statuses may be filtered out.

config_fields.name: campaign_statuses
config_fields.description: Select the statuses you want to be loaded in the stream. If no specific statuses are selected, the API's default behavior applies, and some statuses may be filtered out.

config_fields.description: The Client Id for your OAuth app

config_fields.name: client_secret
config_fields.description: The Client Secret for your OAuth app

config_fields.name: credentials
config_fields.description: Credentials for connecting to the Facebook Marketing API

config_fields.name: custom_insights
config_fields.description: A list which contains ad statistics entries, each entry must have a name and can contains fields, breakdowns or action_breakdowns. Click on "add" to fill this field.

config_fields.name: default_ads_insights_action_breakdowns
config_fields.description: Action breakdowns for the Built-in Ads Insights stream that will be used in the request. You can override default values or remove them to make it empty if needed.

config_fields.name: end_date
config_fields.description: The date until which you'd like to replicate data for all incremental streams, in the format YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00Z. All data generated between the start date and this end date will be replicated. Not setting this option will result in always syncing the latest data.

config_fields.name: fetch_thumbnail_images
config_fields.description: Set to active if you want to fetch the thumbnail_url and store the result in thumbnail_data_url for each Ad Creative.

config_fields.name: include_incrementality
config_fields.description: If enabled, the incrementality attribution window will be included in the action attribution windows for all built-in insight streams. This allows you to retrieve incrementality data for action metrics. See the Facebook Marketing API documentation for more details: https://developers.facebook.com/docs/marketing-api/reference/ads-action-stats/

config_fields.name: insights_job_timeout
config_fields.description: Insights Job Timeout establishes the maximum amount of time (in minutes) of waiting for the report job to complete. When timeout is reached the job is considered failed and we are trying to request smaller amount of data by breaking the job to few smaller ones. If you definitely know that 60 minutes is not enough for your report to be processed then you can decrease the timeout value, so we start breaking job to smaller parts faster.

config_fields.name: insights_lookback_window
config_fields.description: The attribution window. Facebook freezes insight data 28 days after it was generated, which means that all data from the past 28 days may have changed since we last emitted it, so you can retrieve refreshed insights from the past by setting this parameter. If you set a custom lookback window value in Facebook account, please provide the same value here.

config_fields.name: page_size
config_fields.description: Page size used when sending requests to Facebook API to specify number of records per page when response has pagination. Most users do not need to set this field unless they specifically need to tune the connector to address specific issues or use cases.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: The date from which you'd like to replicate data for all incremental streams, in the format YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00Z. If not set then all data will be replicated for usual streams and only last 2 years for insight streams.

access_token.description: The value of the generated access token. From your App’s Dashboard, click on "Marketing API" then "Tools". Select permissions ads_management, ads_read, read_insights, business_management. Then click on "Get token". See the docs for more information.
access_token.title: Access Token

action_breakdowns.title: Action Breakdowns

action_report_time.description: Determines the report time of action stats. For example, if a person saw the ad on Jan 1st but converted on Jan 2nd, when you query the API with action_report_time=impression, you see a conversion on Jan 1st. When you query the API with action_report_time=conversion, you see a conversion on Jan 2nd.
action_report_time.title: Action Report Time

breakdowns.description: A list of chosen breakdowns for breakdowns
breakdowns.title: Breakdowns

end_date.description: The date until which you'd like to replicate data for this stream, in the format YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00Z. All data generated between the start date and this end date will be replicated. Not setting this option will result in always syncing the latest data.
end_date.title: End Date

fields.description: A list of chosen fields for fields parameter
fields.title: Fields

include_incrementality.description: If enabled, the incrementality attribution window will be included in the action attribution windows for this custom insight. This allows you to retrieve incrementality data for action metrics.
include_incrementality.title: Include Incrementality

start_date.description: The date from which you'd like to replicate data for this stream, in the format YYYY-MM-DDT00:00:00Z.
start_date.title: Start Date

time_increment.description: Time window in days by which to aggregate statistics. The sync will be chunked into N day intervals, where N is the number of days you specified. For example, if you set this value to 7, then all statistics will be reported as 7-day aggregates by starting from the start_date. If the start and end dates are October 1st and October 30th, then the connector will output 5 records: 01 - 06, 07 - 13, 14 - 20, 21 - 27, and 28 - 30 (3 days only). The minimum allowed value for this field is 1, and the maximum is 89. Cannot be used together with time_increment_period.
time_increment.title: Time Increment

allOf.description:
Calendar-aligned time periods for InsightConfig.

These produce calendar-aligned date buckets rather than N-day rolling windows:
- daily: equivalent to time_increment=1 (1-day buckets)
- weekly: Monday-through-Sunday calendar weeks
- monthly: calendar month boundaries (1st to last day)
allOf.title: TimeIncrementPeriod

time_increment_period.description: Calendar-aligned aggregation period for statistics. Use this instead of time_increment to produce consistently aligned time buckets regardless of start_date. 'daily' is equivalent to time_increment=1. 'weekly' aligns to Monday-through-Sunday calendar weeks. 'monthly' aligns to calendar month boundaries (1st to last day of each month) and is natively supported by the Facebook API. Cannot be used together with time_increment.
time_increment_period.title: Time Increment Period

If you definitely know that 60 minutes is not enough for your report to be processed then you can decrease the timeout value, so we start breaking job to smaller parts faster.

config_fields.description: How many fake records will be in each page (stream slice), before a state message is emitted?

deletions.description:
This only applies to incremental syncs.
Enabling deletion mode informs your destination of deleted documents.
Disabled - Leave this feature disabled, and ignore deleted documents.
Enabled - Enables this feature. When a document is deleted, the connector exports a record with a "deleted at" column containing the time that the document was deleted.

page_size.description:
The page size used when reading documents from the database. The larger the page size, the faster the connector processes documents. However, if a page is too large, the connector may fail.
Choose your page size based on how large the documents are.
See the docs.

It allows for blocking and hashing individual fields or files from a structured schema. Data can be flattened, typed and deduped depending on the destination.
oneOf.title: Replicate Records

oneOf.description: Copy raw files without parsing their contents. Bits are copied into the destination exactly as they appeared in the source. Recommended for use with unstructured text data, non-text and compressed files.

preserve_directory_structure.description: If enabled, sends subdirectory folder structure along with source file names to the destination. Otherwise, files will be synced by their names only. This option is ignored when file-based replication is not enabled.
preserve_directory_structure.title: Preserve Sub-Directories in File Paths

oneOf.title: Copy Raw Files

sanitize_signed_urls.description: When enabled, removes credential-bearing query parameters from signed URLs in the _ab_source_file_url record field. Only relevant for Service Account authentication.
sanitize_signed_urls.title: Sanitize File URLs

start_date.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00.000000Z. Any file modified before this date will not be replicated.
start_date.title: Start Date

streams.description: Each instance of this configuration defines a stream. Use this to define which files belong in the stream, their format, and how they should be parsed and validated. When sending data to warehouse destination such as Snowflake or BigQuery, each stream is a separate table.

days_to_sync_if_history_is_full.description: When the state history of the file store is full, syncs will only read files that were last modified in the provided day range.
days_to_sync_if_history_is_full.title: Days To Sync If History Is Full

format.description: The configuration options that are used to alter how to read incoming files that deviate from the standard formatting.

double_as_string.description: Whether to convert double fields to strings. This is recommended if you have decimal numbers with a high degree of precision because there can be a loss precision when handling floating point numbers.
double_as_string.title: Convert Double Fields to Strings

delimiter.description: The character delimiting individual cells in the CSV data. This may only be a 1-character string. For tab-delimited data enter '\t'.
delimiter.title: Delimiter

double_quote.description: Whether two quotes in a quoted CSV value denote a single quote in the data.
double_quote.title: Double Quote

encoding.description: The character encoding of the CSV data. Leave blank to default to UTF8. See list of python encodings for allowable options.
encoding.title: Encoding

escape_char.description: The character used for escaping special characters. To disallow escaping, leave this field blank.
escape_char.title: Escape Character

false_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as false values.
false_values.title: False Values

header_definition.description: How headers will be defined. `User Provided` assumes the CSV does not have a header row and uses the headers provided and `Autogenerated` assumes the CSV does not have a header row and the CDK will generate headers using for `f{i}` where `i` is the index starting from 0. Else, the default behavior is to use the header from the CSV file. If a user wants to autogenerate or provide column names for a CSV having headers, they can skip rows.

column_names.description: The column names that will be used while emitting the CSV records
column_names.title: Column Names

header_definition.title: CSV Header Definition

ignore_errors_on_fields_mismatch.description: Whether to ignore errors that occur when the number of fields in the CSV does not match the number of columns in the schema.
ignore_errors_on_fields_mismatch.title: Ignore errors on field mismatch

inference_type.description: How to infer the types of the columns. If none, inference default to strings.
inference_type.title: Inference Type

null_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as null values. For example, if the value 'NA' should be interpreted as null, enter 'NA' in this field.
null_values.title: Null Values

quote_char.description: The character used for quoting CSV values. To disallow quoting, make this field blank.
quote_char.title: Quote Character

skip_rows_after_header.description: The number of rows to skip after the header row.
skip_rows_after_header.title: Skip Rows After Header

skip_rows_before_header.description: The number of rows to skip before the header row. For example, if the header row is on the 3rd row, enter 2 in this field.
skip_rows_before_header.title: Skip Rows Before Header

strings_can_be_null.description: Whether strings can be interpreted as null values. If true, strings that match the null_values set will be interpreted as null. If false, strings that match the null_values set will be interpreted as the string itself.
strings_can_be_null.title: Strings Can Be Null

true_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as true values.
true_values.title: True Values

decimal_as_float.description: Whether to convert decimal fields to floats. There is a loss of precision when converting decimals to floats, so this is not recommended.
decimal_as_float.title: Convert Decimal Fields to Floats

oneOf.description: Extract text from document formats (.pdf, .docx, .md, .pptx) and emit as one record per file.
Process files locally, supporting `fast` and `ocr` modes. This is the default option.
oneOf.title: Local

oneOf.description: Process files via an API, using the `hi_res` mode. This option is useful for increased performance and accuracy, but requires an API key and a hosted instance of unstructured.

api_key.description: The API key to use matching the environment
api_key.title: API Key

api_url.description: The URL of the unstructured API to use
api_url.title: API URL

parameters.description: List of parameters send to the API

name.description: The name of the unstructured API parameter to use
name.title: Parameter name

parameters.title: Additional URL Parameters

oneOf.title: via API

skip_unprocessable_files.description: If true, skip files that cannot be parsed and pass the error message along as the _ab_source_file_parse_error field. If false, fail the sync.
skip_unprocessable_files.title: Skip Unprocessable Files

strategy.description: The strategy used to parse documents. `fast` extracts text directly from the document which doesn't work for all files. `ocr_only` is more reliable, but slower. `hi_res` is the most reliable, but requires an API key and a hosted instance of unstructured and can't be used with local mode. See the unstructured.io documentation for more details: https://unstructured-io.github.io/unstructured/core/partition.html#partition-pdf
strategy.title: Parsing Strategy

oneOf.title: Unstructured Document Format

format.title: Format

globs.description: The pattern used to specify which files should be selected from the file system. For more information on glob pattern matching look here.
globs.title: Globs

input_schema.description: The schema that will be used to validate records extracted from the file. This will override the stream schema that is auto-detected from incoming files.
input_schema.title: Input Schema

legacy_prefix.description: The path prefix configured in v3 versions of the S3 connector. This option is deprecated in favor of a single glob.
legacy_prefix.title: Legacy Prefix

primary_key.description: The column or columns (for a composite key) that serves as the unique identifier of a record. If empty, the primary key will default to the parser's default primary key.
primary_key.title: Primary Key

recent_n_files_to_read_for_schema_discov.description: The number of resent files which will be used to discover the schema for this stream.
recent_n_files_to_read_for_schema_discov.title: Files To Read For Schema Discover

schemaless.description: When enabled, syncs will not validate or structure records against the stream's schema.
schemaless.title: Schemaless

use_first_found_file_for_schema_discover.description: When enabled, the source will use the first found file for schema discovery. Helps to avoid long discovery step.
use_first_found_file_for_schema_discover.title: Use First Found File For Schema Discover

validation_policy.description: The name of the validation policy that dictates sync behavior when a record does not adhere to the stream schema.
validation_policy.title: Validation Policy

streams.title: The list of streams to sync

config_schema.title: Config
