name: GNews

config_fields.name: api_key
config_fields.description: API Key

config_fields.name: country
config_fields.description: This parameter allows you to specify the country where the news articles returned by the API were published, the contents of the articles are not necessarily related to the specified country. You have to set as value the 2 letters code of the country you want to filter.

config_fields.name: end_date
config_fields.description: This parameter allows you to filter the articles that have a publication date smaller than or equal to the  specified value. The date must respect the following format: YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss (in UTC)

config_fields.name: in
config_fields.description: This parameter allows you to choose in which attributes the keywords are searched. The attributes that can be set are title, description and content. It is possible to combine several attributes.

config_fields.name: nullable
config_fields.description: This parameter allows you to specify the attributes that you allow to return null values. The attributes that  can be set are title, description and content. It is possible to combine several attributes

config_fields.name: query
config_fields.description:
This parameter allows you to specify your search keywords to find the news articles you are looking for. The keywords will be used to return the most relevant articles. It is possible to use logical operators  with keywords. - Phrase Search Operator: This operator allows you to make an exact search. Keywords surrounded by 
  quotation marks are used to search for articles with the exact same keyword
sequence. 
  For example the query: "Apple iPhone" will return articles matching at
least once this sequence of keywords. - Logical AND Operator: This operator allows you to make sure that several keywords are all used in the article
  search. By default the space character acts as an AND operator, it is
possible to replace the space character 
  by AND to obtain the same result. For example the query: Apple Microsoft
is equivalent to Apple AND Microsoft - Logical OR Operator: This operator allows you to retrieve articles matching the keyword a or the keyword b.
  It is important to note that this operator has a higher precedence than
the AND operator. For example the 
  query: Apple OR Microsoft will return all articles matching the keyword
Apple as well as all articles matching 
  the keyword Microsoft
- Logical NOT Operator: This operator allows you to remove from the results the articles corresponding to the
  specified keywords. To use it, you need to add NOT in front of each word
or phrase surrounded by quotes.
  For example the query: Apple NOT iPhone will return all articles matching
the keyword Apple but not the keyword
  iPhone

config_fields.name: sortby
config_fields.description:
This parameter allows you to choose with which type of sorting the articles should be returned. Two values  are possible:
  - publishedAt = sort by publication date, the articles with the most recent
publication date are returned first
  - relevance = sort by best match to keywords, the articles with the best
match are returned first

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: This parameter allows you to filter the articles that have a publication date greater than or equal to the  specified value. The date must respect the following format: YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss (in UTC)

config_fields.name: top_headlines_query

  quotation marks are used to search for articles with the exact same keyword
sequence. 
  For example the query: "Apple iPhone" will return articles matching at
least once this sequence of keywords. - Logical AND Operator: This operator allows you to make sure that several keywords are all used in the article
  search. By default the space character acts as an AND operator, it is
possible to replace the space character 
  by AND to obtain the same result. For example the query: Apple Microsoft
is equivalent to Apple AND Microsoft - Logical OR Operator: This operator allows you to retrieve articles matching the keyword a or the keyword b.
  It is important to note that this operator has a higher precedence than
the AND operator. For example the 
  query: Apple OR Microsoft will return all articles matching the keyword
Apple as well as all articles matching 
  the keyword Microsoft
- Logical NOT Operator: This operator allows you to remove from the results the articles corresponding to the
  specified keywords. To use it, you need to add NOT in front of each word
or phrase surrounded by quotes.
  For example the query: Apple NOT iPhone will return all articles matching
the keyword Apple but not the keyword
  iPhone

is equivalent to Apple AND Microsoft - Logical OR Operator: This operator allows you to retrieve articles matching the keyword a or the keyword b.
  It is important to note that this operator has a higher precedence than
the AND operator. For example the 
  query: Apple OR Microsoft will return all articles matching the keyword
Apple as well as all articles matching 
  the keyword Microsoft
- Logical NOT Operator: This operator allows you to remove from the results the articles corresponding to the
  specified keywords. To use it, you need to add NOT in front of each word
or phrase surrounded by quotes.
  For example the query: Apple NOT iPhone will return all articles matching
the keyword Apple but not the keyword
  iPhone
top_headlines_query.title: Top Headlines Query

top_headlines_topic.description: This parameter allows you to change the category for the request.
top_headlines_topic.title: Top Headlines Topic

config_fields.description: Enables conversion of `conversions:*` event metrics from integers to floats. This is beneficial for preventing data rounding when the API returns float values for any `conversions:*` fields.

config_fields.description: Credentials for the service

config_fields.description: The end date from which to replicate report data in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Data generated after this date will not be included in the report. Not applied to custom Cohort reports. When no date is provided or the date is in the future, the date from today is used.

config_fields.name: date_ranges_start_date
config_fields.description: The start date from which to replicate report data in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Data generated before this date will not be included in the report. Not applied to custom Cohort reports.

config_fields.name: keep_empty_rows
config_fields.description: If false, each row with all metrics equal to 0 will not be returned. If true, these rows will be returned if they are not separately removed by a filter. More information is available in the documentation.

config_fields.name: lookback_window
config_fields.description: Since attribution changes after the event date, and Google Analytics has a data processing latency, we should specify how many days in the past we should refresh the data in every run. So if you set it at 5 days, in every sync it will fetch the last bookmark date minus 5 days.

config_fields.name: property_ids
config_fields.description: A list of your Property IDs. The Property ID is a unique number assigned to each property in Google Analytics, found in your GA4 property URL. This ID allows the connector to track the specific events associated with your property. Refer to the Google Analytics documentation to locate your property ID.

config_fields.name: subscription_tier
config_fields.description: Quota tier of the Google Analytics 4 properties being queried. Determines the per-property rate-limit policy applied locally once the tier-aware rate-limit budget is activated. Select "Analytics 360 Property" only if all configured property IDs belong to an Analytics 360 subscription. See https://developers.google.com/analytics/devguides/reporting/data/v1/quotas.

config_fields.name: window_in_days
config_fields.description: The interval in days for each data request made to the Google Analytics API. A larger value speeds up data sync, but increases the chance of data sampling, which may result in inaccuracies. We recommend a value of 1 to minimize sampling, unless speed is an absolute priority over accuracy. Acceptable values range from 1 to 364. Does not apply to custom Cohort reports. More information is available in the documentation.

When sending data to warehouse destination such as Snowflake or BigQuery, each stream is a separate table.

Used during spec; allows the developer to configure the cloud provider specific options
that are needed when users configure a file-based source.

credentials.title: Authentication

oneOf.description: Recommended - Extract and load structured records into your destination of choice. This is the classic method of moving data in Airbyte. It allows for blocking and hashing individual fields or files from a structured schema. Data can be flattened, typed and deduped depending on the destination.
oneOf.title: Replicate Records

oneOf.description: Copy raw files without parsing their contents. Bits are copied into the destination exactly as they appeared in the source. Recommended for use with unstructured text data, non-text and compressed files.

preserve_directory_structure.description: If enabled, sends subdirectory folder structure along with source file names to the destination. Otherwise, files will be synced by their names only. This option is ignored when file-based replication is not enabled.
preserve_directory_structure.title: Preserve Sub-Directories in File Paths

oneOf.title: Copy Raw Files

oneOf.description: Sends one identity stream and one for more permissions (ACL) streams to the destination. This data can be used in downstream systems to recreate permission restrictions mirroring the original source.

domain.description: The Google domain of the identities.
domain.title: Domain

include_identities_stream.description: This data can be used in downstream systems to recreate permission restrictions mirroring the original source
include_identities_stream.title: Include Identity Stream

oneOf.title: Replicate Permissions ACL

folder_url.description: URL for the folder you want to sync. Using individual streams and glob patterns, it's possible to only sync a subset of all files located in the folder.
folder_url.title: Folder Url

start_date.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00.000000Z. Any file modified before this date will not be replicated.
start_date.title: Start Date

streams.description: Each instance of this configuration defines a stream. Use this to define which files belong in the stream, their format, and how they should be parsed and validated. When sending data to warehouse destination such as Snowflake or BigQuery, each stream is a separate table.

days_to_sync_if_history_is_full.description: When the state history of the file store is full, syncs will only read files that were last modified in the provided day range.
days_to_sync_if_history_is_full.title: Days To Sync If History Is Full

format.description: The configuration options that are used to alter how to read incoming files that deviate from the standard formatting.

double_as_string.description: Whether to convert double fields to strings. This is recommended if you have decimal numbers with a high degree of precision because there can be a loss precision when handling floating point numbers.
double_as_string.title: Convert Double Fields to Strings

delimiter.description: The character delimiting individual cells in the CSV data. This may only be a 1-character string. For tab-delimited data enter '\t'.
delimiter.title: Delimiter

double_quote.description: Whether two quotes in a quoted CSV value denote a single quote in the data.
double_quote.title: Double Quote

encoding.description: The character encoding of the CSV data. Leave blank to default to UTF8. See list of python encodings for allowable options.
encoding.title: Encoding

escape_char.description: The character used for escaping special characters. To disallow escaping, leave this field blank.
escape_char.title: Escape Character

false_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as false values.
false_values.title: False Values

header_definition.description: How headers will be defined. `User Provided` assumes the CSV does not have a header row and uses the headers provided and `Autogenerated` assumes the CSV does not have a header row and the CDK will generate headers using for `f{i}` where `i` is the index starting from 0. Else, the default behavior is to use the header from the CSV file. If a user wants to autogenerate or provide column names for a CSV having headers, they can skip rows.

column_names.description: The column names that will be used while emitting the CSV records
column_names.title: Column Names

header_definition.title: CSV Header Definition

ignore_errors_on_fields_mismatch.description: Whether to ignore errors that occur when the number of fields in the CSV does not match the number of columns in the schema.
ignore_errors_on_fields_mismatch.title: Ignore errors on field mismatch

null_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as null values. For example, if the value 'NA' should be interpreted as null, enter 'NA' in this field.
null_values.title: Null Values

quote_char.description: The character used for quoting CSV values. To disallow quoting, make this field blank.
quote_char.title: Quote Character

skip_rows_after_header.description: The number of rows to skip after the header row.
skip_rows_after_header.title: Skip Rows After Header

skip_rows_before_header.description: The number of rows to skip before the header row. For example, if the header row is on the 3rd row, enter 2 in this field.
skip_rows_before_header.title: Skip Rows Before Header

strings_can_be_null.description: Whether strings can be interpreted as null values. If true, strings that match the null_values set will be interpreted as null. If false, strings that match the null_values set will be interpreted as the string itself.
strings_can_be_null.title: Strings Can Be Null

true_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as true values.
true_values.title: True Values

decimal_as_float.description: Whether to convert decimal fields to floats. There is a loss of precision when converting decimals to floats, so this is not recommended.
decimal_as_float.title: Convert Decimal Fields to Floats

oneOf.description: Extract text from document formats (.pdf, .docx, .md, .pptx) and emit as one record per file.
Process files locally, supporting `fast` and `ocr` modes. This is the default option.
oneOf.title: Local

skip_unprocessable_files.description: If true, skip files that cannot be parsed and pass the error message along as the _ab_source_file_parse_error field. If false, fail the sync.
skip_unprocessable_files.title: Skip Unprocessable Files

strategy.description: The strategy used to parse documents. `fast` extracts text directly from the document which doesn't work for all files. `ocr_only` is more reliable, but slower. `hi_res` is the most reliable, but requires an API key and a hosted instance of unstructured and can't be used with local mode. See the unstructured.io documentation for more details: https://unstructured-io.github.io/unstructured/core/partition.html#partition-pdf
strategy.title: Parsing Strategy

oneOf.title: Unstructured Document Format

format.title: Format

globs.description: The pattern used to specify which files should be selected from the file system. For more information on glob pattern matching look here.
globs.title: Globs

input_schema.description: The schema that will be used to validate records extracted from the file. This will override the stream schema that is auto-detected from incoming files.
input_schema.title: Input Schema

primary_key.description: The column or columns (for a composite key) that serves as the unique identifier of a record. If empty, the primary key will default to the parser's default primary key.
primary_key.title: Primary Key

recent_n_files_to_read_for_schema_discov.description: The number of resent files which will be used to discover the schema for this stream.
recent_n_files_to_read_for_schema_discov.title: Files To Read For Schema Discover

schemaless.description: When enabled, syncs will not validate or structure records against the stream's schema.
schemaless.title: Schemaless

use_first_found_file_for_schema_discover.description: When enabled, the source will use the first found file for schema discovery. Helps to avoid long discovery step.
use_first_found_file_for_schema_discover.title: Use First Found File For Schema Discover

validation_policy.description: The name of the validation policy that dictates sync behavior when a record does not adhere to the stream schema.
validation_policy.title: Validation Policy

streams.title: The list of streams to sync

name: Google Search Console

config_fields.name: always_use_aggregation_type_auto
config_fields.description: Some search analytics streams fail with a 400 error if the specified `aggregationType` is not supported. This is customer implementation dependent and if this error is encountered, enable this setting which will override the existing `aggregationType` to use `auto` which should resolve the stream errors.

config_fields.name: data_state
config_fields.description: If set to 'final', the returned data will include only finalized, stable data. If set to 'all', fresh data will be included. When using Incremental sync mode, we do not recommend setting this parameter to 'all' as it may cause data loss. More information can be found in our full documentation.

config_fields.name: end_date
config_fields.description: UTC date in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Any data created after this date will not be replicated. Must be greater or equal to the start date field. Leaving this field blank will replicate all data from the start date onward.

config_fields.name: num_workers
config_fields.description: The number of worker threads to use for the sync. For more details on Google Search Console rate limits, refer to the docs.

config_fields.name: requests_per_minute
config_fields.description: The maximum number of requests per minute for Search Analytics API calls. The default (1200) matches Google's documented maximum quota. If you are experiencing rate limit errors, you may need to lower this value. Most new Google Cloud projects start with a quota of 60 requests per minute. Check your Google Cloud Console quotas to see your actual limit.

name: Google Sheets

config_fields.name: allow_leading_numbers
config_fields.description: Allows column names to start with numbers. Example: "50th Percentile" → "50_th_percentile" This option will only work if "Convert Column Names to SQL-Compliant Format (names_conversion)" is enabled.

config_fields.name: batch_size
config_fields.description: Default value is 1000000. An integer representing row batch size for each sent request to Google Sheets API. Row batch size means how many rows are processed from the google sheet, for example default value 1000000 would process rows 2-1000002, then 1000003-2000003 and so on. Based on Google Sheets API limits documentation, it is possible to send up to 300 requests per minute, but each individual request has to be processed under 180 seconds, otherwise the request returns a timeout error. In regards to this information, consider network speed and number of columns of the google sheet when deciding a batch_size value.

config_fields.name: combine_letter_number_pairs
config_fields.description: Combines adjacent letters and numbers. Example: "Q3 2023" → "q3_2023" This option will only work if "Convert Column Names to SQL-Compliant Format (names_conversion)" is enabled.

config_fields.name: combine_number_word_pairs
config_fields.description: Combines adjacent numbers and words. Example: "50th Percentile?" → "_50th_percentile_" This option will only work if "Convert Column Names to SQL-Compliant Format (names_conversion)" is enabled.

config_fields.name: credentials
config_fields.description: Credentials for connecting to the Google Sheets API

config_fields.name: names_conversion
config_fields.description: Converts column names to a SQL-compliant format (snake_case, lowercase, etc). If enabled, you can further customize the sanitization using the options below.

config_fields.name: num_workers
config_fields.description: Number of concurrent threads for syncing. Higher values can speed up syncs for spreadsheets with multiple sheets, but may hit rate limits. Google Sheets API limits to 300 read requests per minute (~5 req/sec).

config_fields.name: read_empty_header_columns
config_fields.description: When enabled, the connector will continue reading columns after empty header cells and will include data from those columns using generated column names (e.g., "column_C"). By default, the connector stops reading columns when it encounters an empty header cell.

config_fields.name: remove_leading_trailing_underscores
config_fields.description: Removes leading and trailing underscores from column names. Does not remove leading underscores from column names that start with a number. Example: "50th Percentile? "→ "_50_th_percentile" This option will only work if "Convert Column Names to SQL-Compliant Format (names_conversion)" is enabled.

config_fields.name: remove_special_characters
config_fields.description: Removes all special characters from column names. Example: "Example ID*" → "example_id" This option will only work if "Convert Column Names to SQL-Compliant Format (names_conversion)" is enabled.

config_fields.name: spreadsheet_id
config_fields.description: Enter the link to the Google spreadsheet you want to sync. To copy the link, click the 'Share' button in the top-right corner of the spreadsheet, then click 'Copy link'.

config_fields.name: stream_name_overrides
config_fields.description:
**Overridden streams will default to Sync Mode: Full Refresh (Append), which does not support primary keys. If you want to use primary keys and deduplication, update the sync mode to "Full Refresh | Overwrite + Deduped" in your connection settings.**
Allows you to rename streams (Google Sheet tab names) as they appear in Airbyte. 
Each item should be an object with a `source_stream_name` (the exact name of the sheet/tab in your spreadsheet)  and a `custom_stream_name` (the name you want it to appear as in Airbyte and the destination).
If a `source_stream_name` is not found in your spreadsheet, it will be ignored and the default name will be used. This feature only affects stream (sheet/tab) names, not field/column names.
If you want to rename fields or column names, you can do so using the Airbyte Mappings feature after your connection is created. See the Airbyte documentation for more details on how to use Mappings.
Examples:
  - To rename a sheet called "Sheet1" to "sales_data", and "2024 Q1" to "q1_2024":
    [
      { "source_stream_name": "Sheet1", "custom_stream_name": "sales_data" },
      { "source_stream_name": "2024 Q1", "custom_stream_name": "q1_2024" }
    ]
  - If you do not wish to rename any streams, leave this blank.

allow_leading_numbers.description: Allows column names to start with numbers. Example: "50th Percentile" → "50_th_percentile" This option will only work if "Convert Column Names to SQL-Compliant Format (names_conversion)" is enabled.
allow_leading_numbers.title: Allow Leading Numbers

batch_size.description: Default value is 1000000. An integer representing row batch size for each sent request to Google Sheets API. Row batch size means how many rows are processed from the google sheet, for example default value 1000000 would process rows 2-1000002, then 1000003-2000003 and so on. Based on Google Sheets API limits documentation, it is possible to send up to 300 requests per minute, but each individual request has to be processed under 180 seconds, otherwise the request returns a timeout error. In regards to this information, consider network speed and number of columns of the google sheet when deciding a batch_size value.
batch_size.title: Row Batch Size

combine_letter_number_pairs.description: Combines adjacent letters and numbers. Example: "Q3 2023" → "q3_2023" This option will only work if "Convert Column Names to SQL-Compliant Format (names_conversion)" is enabled.
combine_letter_number_pairs.title: Combine Letter-Number Pairs

combine_number_word_pairs.description: Combines adjacent numbers and words. Example: "50th Percentile?" → "_50th_percentile_" This option will only work if "Convert Column Names to SQL-Compliant Format (names_conversion)" is enabled.
combine_number_word_pairs.title: Combine Number-Word Pairs

config_fields.name: author_year_end
config_fields.description: (Optional) Defines the maximum birth year of the authors. Books by authors born after the end year will not be returned. Supports both positive (CE) or negative (BCE) integer values

config_fields.name: author_year_start
config_fields.description: (Optional) Defines the minimum birth year of the authors. Books by authors born prior to the start year will not be returned. Supports both positive (CE) or negative (BCE) integer values

config_fields.name: copyright
config_fields.description: (Optional) Use this to find books with a certain copyright status - true for books with existing copyrights, false for books in the public domain in the USA, or null for books with no available copyright information.

config_fields.name: languages
config_fields.description: (Optional) Use this to find books in any of a list of languages. They must be comma-separated, two-character language codes.

config_fields.name: search
config_fields.description: (Optional) Use this to search author names and book titles with given words. They must be separated by a space (i.e. %20 in URL-encoded format) and are case-insensitive.

config_fields.name: sort
config_fields.description: (Optional) Use this to sort books - ascending for Project Gutenberg ID numbers from lowest to highest, descending for IDs highest to lowest, or popular (the default) for most popular to least popular by number of downloads.

config_fields.name: topic
config_fields.description: (Optional) Use this to search for a case-insensitive key-phrase in books' bookshelves or subjects.

name: HubSpot

config_fields.name: credentials
config_fields.description: Choose how to authenticate to HubSpot.

config_fields.name: enable_experimental_streams
config_fields.description: If enabled then experimental streams become available for sync.

config_fields.name: lookback_window
config_fields.description: How far back (in minutes) to re-fetch records during incremental syncs for CRM Search streams (e.g. contacts, companies, deals, tickets). Set this if you notice missing records in CRM Search streams to recover data that may be delayed by the HubSpot API. Does not affect property history streams.

config_fields.name: num_worker
config_fields.description: The number of worker threads to use for the sync.

config_fields.name: property_history_lookback_window
config_fields.description: How far back (in minutes) to re-fetch records during incremental syncs for property history streams (deals, contacts, companies property history). Set this if you notice missing records in property history streams caused by cursor drift from HubSpot calculated properties.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00Z. Any data before this date will not be replicated. If not set, "2006-06-01T00:00:00Z" (Hubspot creation date) will be used as start date. It's recommended to provide relevant to your data start date value to optimize synchronization.

config_fields.name: treat_numbers_and_booleans_as_strings
config_fields.description: If enabled, HubSpot dynamic `number` and `boolean` properties are exposed as `string`. Useful when HubSpot returns values that do not match the declared type and the destination rejects the records. After changing this setting, refresh the source schema and refresh the affected stream data. See the connector documentation for details.

items.description: Association Between Objects Streams. Associations represent the relationships between objects and activities in the HubSpot CRM.

from_object.description: The name of the object associating.
from_object.title: From Object

stream_name.description: The name to use for the Association Between Objects Stream. If not provided, default name associations_<from_object>_<to_object> will be used.
stream_name.title: Stream Name

to_object.description: The name of the object associating the record to.
to_object.title: To Object

credentials.description: Choose how to authenticate to HubSpot.

access_token.description: HubSpot Access token. See the Hubspot docs if you need help finding this token.
access_token.title: Access token

credentials.title: Authentication

items.description: Association streams involving custom objects. Use this to sync associations where at least one side is a custom object. Custom objects should be referenced by their fullyQualifiedName (e.g., 'p_my_custom_object') or objectTypeId (e.g., '2-12345'). You can find these identifiers in HubSpot Settings or via the /crm/v3/schemas API. Standard object names (e.g., 'contacts', 'companies') can also be used for the other side of the association.

from_object.description: The name or identifier of the source object. For custom objects, use the fullyQualifiedName (e.g., 'p_my_custom_object') or objectTypeId (e.g., '2-12345'). For standard objects, use the object name (e.g., 'contacts', 'companies', 'deals').
from_object.title: From Object

stream_name.description: The name to use for the association stream. If not provided, the default name associations_<from_object>_<to_object> will be used.
stream_name.title: Stream Name

to_object.description: The name or identifier of the target object. For custom objects, use the fullyQualifiedName (e.g., 'p_my_custom_object') or objectTypeId (e.g., '2-12345'). For standard objects, use the object name (e.g., 'contacts', 'companies', 'deals').
to_object.title: To Object

enable_experimental_streams.description: If enabled then experimental streams become available for sync.
enable_experimental_streams.title: Enable experimental streams

lookback_window.description: How far back (in minutes) to re-fetch records during incremental syncs for CRM Search streams (e.g. contacts, companies, deals, tickets). Set this if you notice missing records in CRM Search streams to recover data that may be delayed by the HubSpot API. Does not affect property history streams.
lookback_window.title: CRM Search Lookback Window (minutes)

num_worker.description: The number of worker threads to use for the sync.
num_worker.title: Number of concurrent threads

property_history_lookback_window.description: How far back (in minutes) to re-fetch records during incremental syncs for property history streams (deals, contacts, companies property history). Set this if you notice missing records in property history streams caused by cursor drift from HubSpot calculated properties.
property_history_lookback_window.title: Property History Lookback Window (minutes)

start_date.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00Z. Any data before this date will not be replicated. If not set, "2006-06-01T00:00:00Z" (Hubspot creation date) will be used as start date. It's recommended to provide relevant to your data start date value to optimize synchronization.
start_date.title: Start date

treat_numbers_and_booleans_as_strings.description: If enabled, HubSpot dynamic `number` and `boolean` properties are exposed as `string`. Useful when HubSpot returns values that do not match the declared type and the destination rejects the records. After changing this setting, refresh the source schema and refresh the affected stream data. See the connector documentation for details.

config_fields.name: access_token
config_fields.description: Access token for making authenticated requests. See the Intercom docs for more information.

config_fields.name: activity_logs_time_step
config_fields.description: Set lower value in case of failing long running sync of Activity Logs stream.

config_fields.name: api_rate_limit
config_fields.description: The effective API request budget per minute. The default of 9500 is 95% of the standard Intercom rate limit (10,000/min), leaving headroom for occasional bursts. If your workspace has a higher rate limit (e.g. 150,000/min), set this to ~95% of that value (e.g. 142500) to leverage your full API throughput. A per-10-second window is derived automatically as api_rate_limit / 6.

name: Kafka

config_fields.name: MessageFormat
config_fields.description: The serialization used based on this

config_fields.name: auto_commit_interval_ms
config_fields.description: The frequency in milliseconds that the consumer offsets are auto-committed to Kafka if enable.auto.commit is set to true.

config_fields.name: auto_offset_reset
config_fields.description: What to do when there is no initial offset in Kafka or if the current offset does not exist any more on the server - earliest: automatically reset the offset to the earliest offset, latest: automatically reset the offset to the latest offset, none: throw exception to the consumer if no previous offset is found for the consumer's group, anything else: throw exception to the consumer.

config_fields.name: bootstrap_servers
config_fields.description: A list of host/port pairs to use for establishing the initial connection to the Kafka cluster. The client will make use of all servers irrespective of which servers are specified here for bootstrapping—this list only impacts the initial hosts used to discover the full set of servers. This list should be in the form host1:port1,host2:port2,.... Since these servers are just used for the initial connection to discover the full cluster membership (which may change dynamically), this list need not contain the full set of servers (you may want more than one, though, in case a server is down).

config_fields.name: client_dns_lookup
config_fields.description: Controls how the client uses DNS lookups. If set to use_all_dns_ips, connect to each returned IP address in sequence until a successful connection is established. After a disconnection, the next IP is used. Once all IPs have been used once, the client resolves the IP(s) from the hostname again. If set to resolve_canonical_bootstrap_servers_only, resolve each bootstrap address into a list of canonical names. After the bootstrap phase, this behaves the same as use_all_dns_ips. If set to default (deprecated), attempt to connect to the first IP address returned by the lookup, even if the lookup returns multiple IP addresses.

config_fields.name: client_id
config_fields.description: An ID string to pass to the server when making requests. The purpose of this is to be able to track the source of requests beyond just ip/port by allowing a logical application name to be included in server-side request logging.

config_fields.name: enable_auto_commit
config_fields.description: If true, the consumer's offset will be periodically committed in the background.

config_fields.name: group_id
config_fields.description: The Group ID is how you distinguish different consumer groups.

config_fields.name: max_poll_records
config_fields.description: The maximum number of records returned in a single call to poll(). Note, that max_poll_records does not impact the underlying fetching behavior. The consumer will cache the records from each fetch request and returns them incrementally from each poll.

config_fields.name: max_records_process
config_fields.description: The Maximum to be processed per execution

config_fields.name: polling_time
config_fields.description: Amount of time in milliseconds Kafka connector should try to poll for messages.

config_fields.name: protocol
config_fields.description: The Protocol used to communicate with brokers.

config_fields.name: receive_buffer_bytes
config_fields.description: The size of the TCP receive buffer (SO_RCVBUF) to use when reading data. If the value is -1, the OS default will be used.

config_fields.name: repeated_calls
config_fields.description: The number of repeated calls to poll() if no messages were received.

config_fields.name: request_timeout_ms
config_fields.description: The configuration controls the maximum amount of time the client will wait for the response of a request. If the response is not received before the timeout elapses the client will resend the request if necessary or fail the request if retries are exhausted.

config_fields.name: retry_backoff_ms
config_fields.description: The amount of time to wait before attempting to retry a failed request to a given topic partition. This avoids repeatedly sending requests in a tight loop under some failure scenarios.

config_fields.name: subscription
config_fields.description: You can choose to manually assign a list of partitions, or subscribe to all topics matching specified pattern to get dynamically assigned partitions.

config_fields.name: test_topic
config_fields.description: The Topic to test in case the Airbyte can consume messages.

name: Klaviyo

config_fields.name: api_key
config_fields.description: Klaviyo API Key. See our docs if you need help finding this key.

config_fields.name: disable_fetching_predictive_analytics
config_fields.description: Certain streams like the profiles stream can retrieve predictive analytics data from Klaviyo's API. However, at high volume, this can lead to service availability issues on the API which can be improved by not fetching this field. WARNING: Enabling this setting will stop the  "predictive_analytics" column from being populated in your downstream destination.

config_fields.name: lookback_window
config_fields.description: The number of days to look back when syncing data in incremental mode. This helps capture any late-arriving data. Only applies to the events_detailed stream.

config_fields.name: metric_ids
config_fields.description: OPTIONAL: Comma-separated list of specific metric IDs to use for flow_series_reports and campaign_values_reports streams. If left empty, the connector will automatically fetch reports for ALL available metrics in your account. Due to Klaviyo's strict API rate limits - see [Klaviyo's API docs](https://developers.klaviyo.com/en/reference/query_campaign_values), syncing all metrics can be extremely slow and may take hours to complete. RECOMMENDED: Specify only the conversion metrics you need (e.g., "RESQ6t" for Placed Order) to avoid slow syncs. Find metric IDs in your Klaviyo account under Analytics > Metrics, or use the metrics stream to list all available metrics and their IDs.

config_fields.name: num_workers
config_fields.description: The number of worker threads to use for the sync. The performance upper boundary is based on the limit of your Klaviyo plan. More info about the rate limit plan tiers can be found on Klaviyo's API docs.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00Z. Any data before this date will not be replicated. This field is optional - if not provided, all data will be replicated.

api_key.description: Klaviyo API Key. See our docs if you need help finding this key.
api_key.title: Api Key

disable_fetching_predictive_analytics.description: Certain streams like the profiles stream can retrieve predictive analytics data from Klaviyo's API. However, at high volume, this can lead to service availability issues on the API which can be improved by not fetching this field. WARNING: Enabling this setting will stop the  "predictive_analytics" column from being populated in your downstream destination.
disable_fetching_predictive_analytics.title: Disable Fetching Predictive Analytics

lookback_window.description: The number of days to look back when syncing data in incremental mode. This helps capture any late-arriving data. Only applies to the events_detailed stream.
lookback_window.title: Lookback Window (Days)

metric_ids.description: OPTIONAL: Comma-separated list of specific metric IDs to use for flow_series_reports and campaign_values_reports streams. If left empty, the connector will automatically fetch reports for ALL available metrics in your account. Due to Klaviyo's strict API rate limits - see [Klaviyo's API docs](https://developers.klaviyo.com/en/reference/query_campaign_values), syncing all metrics can be extremely slow and may take hours to complete. RECOMMENDED: Specify only the conversion metrics you need (e.g., "RESQ6t" for Placed Order) to avoid slow syncs. Find metric IDs in your Klaviyo account under Analytics > Metrics, or use the metrics stream to list all available metrics and their IDs.
metric_ids.title: Report Stream Conversion Metric IDs

num_workers.description: The number of worker threads to use for the sync. The performance upper boundary is based on the limit of your Klaviyo plan. More info about the rate limit plan tiers can be found on Klaviyo's API docs.
num_workers.title: Number of concurrent threads

config_fields.name: credentials
config_fields.description: Choose how to authenticate to Linear.

config_fields.name: num_workers
config_fields.description: Number of worker threads used to read streams in parallel. Higher values can speed up syncs but increase the risk of hitting Linear's rate limits. The Linear API limits per-user requests by authentication type: API key auth allows 2,500 requests/hour, OAuth allows 5,000 requests/hour, and workspace OAuth scales dynamically with paid seats. Stay at the default unless you are on OAuth and have observed headroom in your rate-limit usage. See Linear API rate limits for details.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the ISO 8601 format (e.g. 2020-01-01T00:00:00.000Z). Any records updated before this date will not be replicated. Only applies to streams that support incremental sync. If unset, defaults to two years ago.

config_fields.description:
To generate your session token, you need to run the following command: ``` curl -X POST \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"username": "person@metabase.com", "password": "fakepassword"}' \
  http://localhost:3000/api/session
``` Then copy the value of the `id` field returned by a successful call to that API.
Note that by default, sessions are good for 14 days and needs to be regenerated.

start_date.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00.000000Z. Any file modified before this date will not be replicated.
start_date.title: Start Date

streams.description: Each instance of this configuration defines a stream. Use this to define which files belong in the stream, their format, and how they should be parsed and validated. When sending data to warehouse destination such as Snowflake or BigQuery, each stream is a separate table.

days_to_sync_if_history_is_full.description: When the state history of the file store is full, syncs will only read files that were last modified in the provided day range.
days_to_sync_if_history_is_full.title: Days To Sync If History Is Full

format.description: The configuration options that are used to alter how to read incoming files that deviate from the standard formatting.

double_as_string.description: Whether to convert double fields to strings. This is recommended if you have decimal numbers with a high degree of precision because there can be a loss precision when handling floating point numbers.
double_as_string.title: Convert Double Fields to Strings

delimiter.description: The character delimiting individual cells in the CSV data. This may only be a 1-character string. For tab-delimited data enter '\t'.
delimiter.title: Delimiter

double_quote.description: Whether two quotes in a quoted CSV value denote a single quote in the data.
double_quote.title: Double Quote

encoding.description: The character encoding of the CSV data. Leave blank to default to UTF8. See list of python encodings for allowable options.
encoding.title: Encoding

escape_char.description: The character used for escaping special characters. To disallow escaping, leave this field blank.
escape_char.title: Escape Character

false_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as false values.
false_values.title: False Values

header_definition.description: How headers will be defined. `User Provided` assumes the CSV does not have a header row and uses the headers provided and `Autogenerated` assumes the CSV does not have a header row and the CDK will generate headers using for `f{i}` where `i` is the index starting from 0. Else, the default behavior is to use the header from the CSV file. If a user wants to autogenerate or provide column names for a CSV having headers, they can skip rows.

column_names.description: The column names that will be used while emitting the CSV records
column_names.title: Column Names

header_definition.title: CSV Header Definition

ignore_errors_on_fields_mismatch.description: Whether to ignore errors that occur when the number of fields in the CSV does not match the number of columns in the schema.
ignore_errors_on_fields_mismatch.title: Ignore errors on field mismatch

null_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as null values. For example, if the value 'NA' should be interpreted as null, enter 'NA' in this field.
null_values.title: Null Values

quote_char.description: The character used for quoting CSV values. To disallow quoting, make this field blank.
quote_char.title: Quote Character

skip_rows_after_header.description: The number of rows to skip after the header row.
skip_rows_after_header.title: Skip Rows After Header

skip_rows_before_header.description: The number of rows to skip before the header row. For example, if the header row is on the 3rd row, enter 2 in this field.
skip_rows_before_header.title: Skip Rows Before Header

strings_can_be_null.description: Whether strings can be interpreted as null values. If true, strings that match the null_values set will be interpreted as null. If false, strings that match the null_values set will be interpreted as the string itself.
strings_can_be_null.title: Strings Can Be Null

true_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as true values.
true_values.title: True Values

decimal_as_float.description: Whether to convert decimal fields to floats. There is a loss of precision when converting decimals to floats, so this is not recommended.
decimal_as_float.title: Convert Decimal Fields to Floats

oneOf.description: Extract text from document formats (.pdf, .docx, .md, .pptx) and emit as one record per file.
Process files locally, supporting `fast` and `ocr` modes. This is the default option.
oneOf.title: Local

skip_unprocessable_files.description: If true, skip files that cannot be parsed and pass the error message along as the _ab_source_file_parse_error field. If false, fail the sync.
skip_unprocessable_files.title: Skip Unprocessable Files

strategy.description: The strategy used to parse documents. `fast` extracts text directly from the document which doesn't work for all files. `ocr_only` is more reliable, but slower. `hi_res` is the most reliable, but requires an API key and a hosted instance of unstructured and can't be used with local mode. See the unstructured.io documentation for more details: https://unstructured-io.github.io/unstructured/core/partition.html#partition-pdf
strategy.title: Parsing Strategy

oneOf.title: Unstructured Document Format

format.title: Format

globs.description: The pattern used to specify which files should be selected from the file system. For more information on glob pattern matching look here.
globs.title: Globs

input_schema.description: The schema that will be used to validate records extracted from the file. This will override the stream schema that is auto-detected from incoming files.
input_schema.title: Input Schema

primary_key.description: The column or columns (for a composite key) that serves as the unique identifier of a record. If empty, the primary key will default to the parser's default primary key.
primary_key.title: Primary Key

schemaless.description: When enabled, syncs will not validate or structure records against the stream's schema.
schemaless.title: Schemaless

validation_policy.description: The name of the validation policy that dictates sync behavior when a record does not adhere to the stream schema.
validation_policy.title: Validation Policy

name: Mixpanel

config_fields.name: attribution_window
config_fields.description: A period of time for attributing results to ads and the lookback period after those actions occur during which ad results are counted. Default attribution window is 5 days. (This value should be non-negative integer)

config_fields.name: credentials
config_fields.description: Choose how to authenticate to Mixpanel

config_fields.name: date_window_size
config_fields.description: Defines window size in days, that used to slice through data. You can reduce it, if amount of data in each window is too big for your environment. (This value should be positive integer)

config_fields.name: end_date
config_fields.description: The date in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Any data after this date will not be replicated. Left empty to always sync to most recent date

config_fields.name: export_lookback_window
config_fields.description: The number of seconds to look back from the last synced timestamp during incremental syncs of the Export stream. This ensures no data is missed due to delays in event recording. Default is 0 seconds. Must be a non-negative integer.

config_fields.name: num_workers
config_fields.description: The number of worker threads to use for the sync. The performance upper boundary is based on the limit of your Mixpanel pricing plan. More info about the rate limit tiers can be found on Mixpanel's API docs.

config_fields.name: page_size
config_fields.description: The number of records to fetch per request for the engage stream. Default is 1000. If you are experiencing long sync times with this stream, try increasing this value.

config_fields.name: project_timezone
config_fields.description: Time zone in which integer date times are stored. The project timezone may be found in the project settings in the Mixpanel console.

config_fields.name: region
config_fields.description: The region of mixpanel domain instance either US or EU.

config_fields.name: select_properties_by_default
config_fields.description: Setting this config parameter to TRUE ensures that new properties on events and engage records are captured. Otherwise new properties will be ignored.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: The date in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Any data before this date will not be replicated. If this option is not set, the connector will replicate data from up to one year ago by default.

name: MongoDb

config_fields.name: database_config
config_fields.description: Configures the MongoDB cluster type.

config_fields.name: discover_sample_size
config_fields.description: The maximum number of documents to sample when attempting to discover the unique fields for a collection.

config_fields.name: discover_timeout_seconds
config_fields.description: The amount of time the connector will wait when it discovers a document. Defaults to 600 seconds. Valid range: 5 seconds to 1200 seconds.

config_fields.name: initial_load_timeout_hours
config_fields.description: The amount of time an initial load is allowed to continue for before catching up on CDC logs.

config_fields.name: initial_waiting_seconds
config_fields.description: The amount of time the connector will wait when it launches to determine if there is new data to sync or not. Defaults to 300 seconds. Valid range: 120 seconds to 1200 seconds.

config_fields.name: invalid_cdc_cursor_position_behavior
config_fields.description: Determines whether Airbyte should fail or re-sync data in case of an stale/invalid cursor value into the WAL. If 'Fail sync' is chosen, a user will have to manually reset the connection before being able to continue syncing data. If 'Re-sync data' is chosen, Airbyte will automatically trigger a refresh but could lead to higher cloud costs and data loss.

config_fields.name: queue_size
config_fields.description: The size of the internal queue. This may interfere with memory consumption and efficiency of the connector, please be careful.

config_fields.name: update_capture_mode
config_fields.description: Determines how Airbyte looks up the value of an updated document. If 'Lookup' is chosen, the current value of the document will be read. If 'Post Image' is chosen, then the version of the document immediately after an update will be read. WARNING : Severe data loss will occur if this option is chosen and the appropriate settings are not set on your Mongo instance : https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/changeStreams/#change-streams-with-document-pre-and-post-images.

discover_timeout_seconds.title: Document discovery timeout in seconds (Advanced)

initial_load_timeout_hours.description: The amount of time an initial load is allowed to continue for before catching up on CDC logs.
initial_load_timeout_hours.title: Initial Load Timeout in Hours (Advanced)

initial_waiting_seconds.description: The amount of time the connector will wait when it launches to determine if there is new data to sync or not. Defaults to 300 seconds. Valid range: 120 seconds to 1200 seconds.
initial_waiting_seconds.title: Initial Waiting Time in Seconds (Advanced)

invalid_cdc_cursor_position_behavior.description: Determines whether Airbyte should fail or re-sync data in case of an stale/invalid cursor value into the WAL. If 'Fail sync' is chosen, a user will have to manually reset the connection before being able to continue syncing data. If 'Re-sync data' is chosen, Airbyte will automatically trigger a refresh but could lead to higher cloud costs and data loss.
invalid_cdc_cursor_position_behavior.title: Invalid CDC position behavior (Advanced)

queue_size.description: The size of the internal queue. This may interfere with memory consumption and efficiency of the connector, please be careful.
queue_size.title: Size of the queue (Advanced)

update_capture_mode.description: Determines how Airbyte looks up the value of an updated document. If 'Lookup' is chosen, the current value of the document will be read. If 'Post Image' is chosen, then the version of the document immediately after an update will be read. WARNING : Severe data loss will occur if this option is chosen and the appropriate settings are not set on your Mongo instance : https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/changeStreams/#change-streams-with-document-pre-and-post-images.
update_capture_mode.title: Capture mode (Advanced)

name: Nasa

config_fields.name: api_key
config_fields.description: API access key used to retrieve data from the NASA APOD API.

config_fields.name: concept_tags
config_fields.description: Indicates whether concept tags should be returned with the rest of the response.  The concept tags are not necessarily included in the explanation, but rather derived from common search tags that are associated with the description text. (Better than just pure text search.) Defaults to False.

config_fields.name: count
config_fields.description: A positive integer, no greater than 100. If this is specified then `count` randomly  chosen images will be returned in a JSON array. Cannot be used in conjunction with  `date` or `start_date` and `end_date`.

config_fields.name: end_date
config_fields.description: Indicates that end of a date range. If `start_date` is specified without an `end_date` then `end_date` defaults to the current date.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: Indicates the start of a date range. All images in the range from `start_date` to  `end_date` will be returned in a JSON array. Must be after 1995-06-16, the first day an APOD picture was posted. There are no images for tomorrow available through this API.

config_fields.name: thumbs
config_fields.description: Indicates whether the API should return a thumbnail image URL for video files. If set to True, the API returns URL of video thumbnail. If an APOD is not a video, this parameter is ignored.

api_key.description: API access key used to retrieve data from the NASA APOD API.

concept_tags.description: Indicates whether concept tags should be returned with the rest of the response.  The concept tags are not necessarily included in the explanation, but rather derived from common search tags that are associated with the description text. (Better than just pure text search.) Defaults to False.

count.description: A positive integer, no greater than 100. If this is specified then `count` randomly  chosen images will be returned in a JSON array. Cannot be used in conjunction with  `date` or `start_date` and `end_date`.

end_date.description: Indicates that end of a date range. If `start_date` is specified without an `end_date` then `end_date` defaults to the current date.

start_date.description: Indicates the start of a date range. All images in the range from `start_date` to  `end_date` will be returned in a JSON array. Must be after 1995-06-16, the first day an APOD picture was posted. There are no images for tomorrow available through this API.

thumbs.description: Indicates whether the API should return a thumbnail image URL for video files. If set to True, the API returns URL of video thumbnail. If an APOD is not a video, this parameter is ignored.

name: News Api

config_fields.name: api_key
config_fields.description: API Key

config_fields.name: category
config_fields.description: The category you want to get top headlines for.

config_fields.name: country
config_fields.description:
The 2-letter ISO 3166-1 code of the country you want to get headlines
for. You can't mix this with the sources parameter.

config_fields.name: domains
config_fields.description:
A comma-seperated string of domains (eg bbc.co.uk, techcrunch.com,
engadget.com) to restrict the search to.

config_fields.name: end_date
config_fields.description:
A date and optional time for the newest article allowed. This should
be in ISO 8601 format.

config_fields.name: exclude_domains
config_fields.description:
A comma-seperated string of domains (eg bbc.co.uk, techcrunch.com,
engadget.com) to remove from the results.

config_fields.name: language
config_fields.description:
The 2-letter ISO-639-1 code of the language you want to get headlines
for. Possible options: ar de en es fr he it nl no pt ru se ud zh.

config_fields.name: search_in
config_fields.description:
Where to apply search query. Possible values are: title, description,
content.

config_fields.name: search_query
config_fields.description:
Search query. See https://newsapi.org/docs/endpoints/everything for 
information.

config_fields.name: sort_by
config_fields.description:
The order to sort the articles in. Possible options: relevancy,
popularity, publishedAt.

config_fields.name: sources
config_fields.description:
Identifiers (maximum 20) for the news sources or blogs you want
headlines from. Use the `/sources` endpoint to locate these
programmatically or look at the sources index:
https://newsapi.com/sources. Will override both country and category.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description:
A date and optional time for the oldest article allowed. This should
be in ISO 8601 format.

name: Newsdata

config_fields.name: api_key
config_fields.description: API Key

config_fields.name: category
config_fields.description: Categories (maximum 5) to restrict the search to.

config_fields.name: country
config_fields.description: 2-letter ISO 3166-1 countries (maximum 5) to restrict the search to.

config_fields.name: domain
config_fields.description: Domains (maximum 5) to restrict the search to. Use the sources stream to find top sources id.

config_fields.name: language
config_fields.description: Languages (maximum 5) to restrict the search to.

query.description:
Keywords or phrases to search for in the news title and content. Advanced Search options:
  - Search `Social`: query = "social"
  - Search `Social Pizza`: query = "social pizza"
  - Search `Social` but not with `pizza`: query = "social -pizza"
  - Search `Social` but not with `pizza` and `wildfire`: query = "social
-pizza -wildfire"
  - Search `Social` and `pizza`: query = "social AND pizza"
  - Search `Social` and `pizza` and `pasta`: query = "social AND pizza
AND pasta"
  - Search `Social` or `pizza`: query = "social OR pizza"
  - Search `Social` or `pizza` but not `pasta`: query = "social OR pizza
-pasta"
  - Search `Social` or `pizza` or `pasta`: query = "social OR pizza OR
pasta" Note: You can't use AND and OR in the same query.

query_in_title.description: Same as `query`, but restricting the search to only the news title. It cannot be used along with `query`.

api_key.description: API Key
api_key.title: API Key

language.description: Languages (maximum 5) to restrict the search to.

invalid_cdc_cursor_position_behavior.description: Determines whether Airbyte should fail or re-sync data in case of an stale/invalid cursor value in the mined logs. If 'Fail sync' is chosen, a user will have to manually reset the connection before being able to continue syncing data. If 'Re-sync data' is chosen, Airbyte will automatically trigger a refresh but could lead to higher cloud costs and data loss.
invalid_cdc_cursor_position_behavior.title: Invalid CDC Position Behavior (Advanced)

lsn_commit_behaviour.default: After loading Data in the destination
lsn_commit_behaviour.description: Determines when Airbyte should flush the LSN of processed WAL logs in the source database. `After loading Data in the destination` is default. If `While reading Data` is selected, in case of a downstream failure (while loading data into the destination), next sync would result in a full sync.

config_fields.name: address
config_fields.description: The full address of the property, in the format of Street, City, State, Zip. Used to retrieve data for a specific property, or together with the radius parameter to search for listings in a specific area

config_fields.name: bath_rooms
config_fields.description: The number of bathrooms, used to search for listings matching this criteria. Supports fractions to indicate partial bathrooms

config_fields.name: bedrooms
config_fields.description: The number of bedrooms, used to search for listings matching this criteria. Use 0 to indicate a studio layout

config_fields.description: The time range for historical record entries, in months. Defaults to 12 if not provided

config_fields.name: latitude
config_fields.description: The latitude of the search area. Use the latitude/longitude and radius parameters to search for listings in a specific area

config_fields.name: longitude

config_fields.name: radius

config_fields.name: state

config_fields.name: status

config_fields.name: bucket
config_fields.description: Name of the S3 bucket where the file(s) exist.

config_fields.description: Deprecated and will be removed soon. Please do not use this field anymore and use streams.name instead. The name of the stream you would like this source to output. Can contain letters, numbers, or underscores.

config_fields.description: Endpoint to an S3 compatible service. Leave empty to use AWS.

config_fields.name: format
config_fields.description: Deprecated and will be removed soon. Please do not use this field anymore and use streams.format instead. The format of the files you'd like to replicate

config_fields.name: path_pattern
config_fields.description: Deprecated and will be removed soon. Please do not use this field anymore and use streams.globs instead. A regular expression which tells the connector which files to replicate. All files which match this pattern will be replicated. Use | to separate multiple patterns. See this page to understand pattern syntax (GLOBSTAR and SPLIT flags are enabled). Use pattern ** to pick up all files.

config_fields.name: provider
config_fields.description: Deprecated and will be removed soon. Please do not use this field anymore and use bucket, aws_access_key_id, aws_secret_access_key and endpoint instead. Use this to load files from S3 or S3-compatible services

config_fields.name: region_name
config_fields.description: AWS region where the S3 bucket is located. If not provided, the region will be determined automatically.

config_fields.name: role_arn
config_fields.description: Specifies the Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of an IAM role that you want to use to perform operations requested using this profile. Set the External ID to the Airbyte workspace ID, which can be found in the URL of this page.

config_fields.name: schema
config_fields.description: Deprecated and will be removed soon. Please do not use this field anymore and use streams.input_schema instead. Optionally provide a schema to enforce, as a valid JSON string. Ensure this is a mapping of { "column" : "type" }, where types are valid JSON Schema datatypes. Leave as {} to auto-infer the schema.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00.000000Z. Any file modified before this date will not be replicated.

config_fields.name: streams
config_fields.description: Each instance of this configuration defines a stream. Use this to define which files belong in the stream, their format, and how they should be parsed and validated. When sending data to warehouse destination such as Snowflake or BigQuery, each stream is a separate table.

config_schema.description:
NOTE: When this Spec is changed, legacy_config_transformer.py must also be modified to uptake the changes
because it is responsible for converting legacy S3 v3 configs into v4 configs using the File-Based CDK.

dataset.title: Output Stream Name

oneOf.description: Recommended - Extract and load structured records into your destination of choice. This is the classic method of moving data in Airbyte. It allows for blocking and hashing individual fields or files from a structured schema. Data can be flattened, typed and deduped depending on the destination.
oneOf.title: Replicate Records

oneOf.description: Copy raw files without parsing their contents. Bits are copied into the destination exactly as they appeared in the source. Recommended for use with unstructured text data, non-text and compressed files.

preserve_directory_structure.description: If enabled, sends subdirectory folder structure along with source file names to the destination. Otherwise, files will be synced by their names only. This option is ignored when file-based replication is not enabled.
preserve_directory_structure.title: Preserve Sub-Directories in File Paths

oneOf.title: Copy Raw Files

endpoint.description: Endpoint to an S3 compatible service. Leave empty to use AWS.

oneOf.description: This connector utilises PyArrow (Apache Arrow) for CSV parsing.

additional_reader_options.description: Optionally add a valid JSON string here to provide additional options to the csv reader. Mappings must correspond to options detailed here. 'column_types' is used internally to handle schema so overriding that would likely cause problems.
additional_reader_options.title: Additional Reader Options

advanced_options.description: Optionally add a valid JSON string here to provide additional Pyarrow ReadOptions. Specify 'column_names' here if your CSV doesn't have header, or if you want to use custom column names. 'block_size' and 'encoding' are already used above, specify them again here will override the values above.
advanced_options.title: Advanced Options

block_size.description: The chunk size in bytes to process at a time in memory from each file. If your data is particularly wide and failing during schema detection, increasing this should solve it. Beware of raising this too high as you could hit OOM errors.
block_size.title: Block Size

delimiter.description: The character delimiting individual cells in the CSV data. This may only be a 1-character string. For tab-delimited data enter '\t'.
delimiter.title: Delimiter

double_quote.description: Whether two quotes in a quoted CSV value denote a single quote in the data.
double_quote.title: Double Quote

encoding.description: The character encoding of the CSV data. Leave blank to default to UTF8. See list of python encodings for allowable options.
encoding.title: Encoding

escape_char.description: The character used for escaping special characters. To disallow escaping, leave this field blank.
escape_char.title: Escape Character

infer_datatypes.description: Configures whether a schema for the source should be inferred from the current data or not. If set to false and a custom schema is set, then the manually enforced schema is used. If a schema is not manually set, and this is set to false, then all fields will be read as strings
infer_datatypes.title: Infer Datatypes

newlines_in_values.description: Whether newline characters are allowed in CSV values. Turning this on may affect performance. Leave blank to default to False.
newlines_in_values.title: Allow newlines in values

quote_char.description: The character used for quoting CSV values. To disallow quoting, make this field blank.
quote_char.title: Quote Character

oneOf.title: CSV

oneOf.description: This connector utilises PyArrow (Apache Arrow) for Parquet parsing.

batch_size.description: Maximum number of records per batch read from the input files. Batches may be smaller if there aren’t enough rows in the file. This option can help avoid out-of-memory errors if your data is particularly wide.
batch_size.title: Record batch size

buffer_size.description: Perform read buffering when deserializing individual column chunks. By default every group column will be loaded fully to memory. This option can help avoid out-of-memory errors if your data is particularly wide.
buffer_size.title: Buffer Size

columns.description: If you only want to sync a subset of the columns from the file(s), add the columns you want here as a comma-delimited list. Leave it empty to sync all columns.
columns.title: Selected Columns

double_as_string.description: Whether to convert double fields to strings. This is recommended if you have decimal numbers with a high degree of precision because there can be a loss precision when handling floating point numbers.
double_as_string.title: Convert Double Fields to Strings

delimiter.description: The character delimiting individual cells in the CSV data. This may only be a 1-character string. For tab-delimited data enter '\t'.
delimiter.title: Delimiter

double_quote.description: Whether two quotes in a quoted CSV value denote a single quote in the data.
double_quote.title: Double Quote

encoding.description: The character encoding of the CSV data. Leave blank to default to UTF8. See list of python encodings for allowable options.
encoding.title: Encoding

escape_char.description: The character used for escaping special characters. To disallow escaping, leave this field blank.
escape_char.title: Escape Character

false_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as false values.
false_values.title: False Values

header_definition.description: How headers will be defined. `User Provided` assumes the CSV does not have a header row and uses the headers provided and `Autogenerated` assumes the CSV does not have a header row and the CDK will generate headers using for `f{i}` where `i` is the index starting from 0. Else, the default behavior is to use the header from the CSV file. If a user wants to autogenerate or provide column names for a CSV having headers, they can skip rows.

column_names.description: The column names that will be used while emitting the CSV records
column_names.title: Column Names

header_definition.title: CSV Header Definition

ignore_errors_on_fields_mismatch.description: Whether to ignore errors that occur when the number of fields in the CSV does not match the number of columns in the schema.
ignore_errors_on_fields_mismatch.title: Ignore errors on field mismatch

inference_type.description: How to infer the types of the columns. If none, inference default to strings.
inference_type.title: Inference Type

null_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as null values. For example, if the value 'NA' should be interpreted as null, enter 'NA' in this field.
null_values.title: Null Values

quote_char.description: The character used for quoting CSV values. To disallow quoting, make this field blank.
quote_char.title: Quote Character

skip_rows_after_header.description: The number of rows to skip after the header row.
skip_rows_after_header.title: Skip Rows After Header

skip_rows_before_header.description: The number of rows to skip before the header row. For example, if the header row is on the 3rd row, enter 2 in this field.
skip_rows_before_header.title: Skip Rows Before Header

strings_can_be_null.description: Whether strings can be interpreted as null values. If true, strings that match the null_values set will be interpreted as null. If false, strings that match the null_values set will be interpreted as the string itself.
strings_can_be_null.title: Strings Can Be Null

true_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as true values.
true_values.title: True Values

decimal_as_float.description: Whether to convert decimal fields to floats. There is a loss of precision when converting decimals to floats, so this is not recommended.
decimal_as_float.title: Convert Decimal Fields to Floats

oneOf.description: Extract text from document formats (.pdf, .docx, .md, .pptx) and emit as one record per file.
Process files locally, supporting `fast` and `ocr` modes. This is the default option.
oneOf.title: Local

skip_unprocessable_files.description: If true, skip files that cannot be parsed and pass the error message along as the _ab_source_file_parse_error field. If false, fail the sync.
skip_unprocessable_files.title: Skip Unprocessable Files

strategy.description: The strategy used to parse documents. `fast` extracts text directly from the document which doesn't work for all files. `ocr_only` is more reliable, but slower. `hi_res` is the most reliable, but requires an API key and a hosted instance of unstructured and can't be used with local mode. See the unstructured.io documentation for more details: https://unstructured-io.github.io/unstructured/core/partition.html#partition-pdf
strategy.title: Parsing Strategy

oneOf.title: Unstructured Document Format

format.title: Format

globs.description: The pattern used to specify which files should be selected from the file system. For more information on glob pattern matching look here.
globs.title: Globs

input_schema.description: The schema that will be used to validate records extracted from the file. This will override the stream schema that is auto-detected from incoming files.
input_schema.title: Input Schema

legacy_prefix.description: The path prefix configured in v3 versions of the S3 connector. This option is deprecated in favor of a single glob.
legacy_prefix.title: Legacy Prefix

primary_key.description: The column or columns (for a composite key) that serves as the unique identifier of a record. If empty, the primary key will default to the parser's default primary key.
primary_key.title: Primary Key

recent_n_files_to_read_for_schema_discov.description: The number of resent files which will be used to discover the schema for this stream.
recent_n_files_to_read_for_schema_discov.title: Files To Read For Schema Discover

schemaless.description: When enabled, syncs will not validate or structure records against the stream's schema.
schemaless.title: Schemaless

skip_full_check_for_parquet.description: When enabled, the CHECK operation for Parquet streams will verify file accessibility but skip the full record-parse step. This avoids out-of-memory errors on large Parquet files.
skip_full_check_for_parquet.title: Skip Full Check for Parquet

use_first_found_file_for_schema_discover.description: When enabled, the source will use the first found file for schema discovery. Helps to avoid long discovery step.
use_first_found_file_for_schema_discover.title: Use First Found File For Schema Discover

validation_policy.description: The name of the validation policy that dictates sync behavior when a record does not adhere to the stream schema.
validation_policy.title: Validation Policy

streams.title: The list of streams to sync

config_schema.title: Config

name: Salesforce

config_fields.name: client_id
config_fields.description: Enter your Salesforce developer application's Client ID

config_fields.name: client_secret
config_fields.description: Enter your Salesforce developer application's Client secret

config_fields.name: force_use_bulk_api
config_fields.description: Toggle to use Bulk API (this might cause empty fields for some streams)

config_fields.name: is_sandbox
config_fields.description: Toggle if you're using a Salesforce Sandbox

config_fields.name: lookback_window
config_fields.description: The duration (ISO8601 duration) to re-read data from the source when running incremental syncs. This compensates for records that may not be immediately available when querying the Salesforce API due to eventual consistency delays. The connector will re-query this amount of time before the last cursor position on each sync. Increase this value if you observe missing records in your destination. See Limitations & Troubleshooting for details.

config_fields.name: refresh_token
config_fields.description: Enter your application's Salesforce Refresh Token used for Airbyte to access your Salesforce account.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: Enter the date (or date-time) in the YYYY-MM-DD or YYYY-MM-DDTHH:mm:ssZ format. Airbyte will replicate the data updated on and after this date. If this field is blank, Airbyte will replicate the data for last two years.

config_fields.name: stream_slice_step
config_fields.description: The size of the time window (ISO8601 duration) to slice requests.

config_fields.name: streams_criteria
config_fields.description: Add filters to select only required stream based on `SObject` name. Use this field to filter which tables are displayed by this connector. This is useful if your Salesforce account has a large number of tables (>1000), in which case you may find it easier to navigate the UI and speed up the connector's performance if you restrict the tables displayed by this connector.

is_sandbox.description: Toggle if you're using a Salesforce Sandbox
is_sandbox.title: Sandbox

lookback_window.description: The duration (ISO8601 duration) to re-read data from the source when running incremental syncs. This compensates for records that may not be immediately available when querying the Salesforce API due to eventual consistency delays. The connector will re-query this amount of time before the last cursor position on each sync. Increase this value if you observe missing records in your destination. See Limitations & Troubleshooting for details.
lookback_window.title: Lookback Window for Incremental sync

refresh_token.description: Enter your application's Salesforce Refresh Token used for Airbyte to access your Salesforce account.
refresh_token.title: Refresh Token
