name: SAP HANA

config_fields.name: check_privileges
config_fields.description: When this feature is enabled, during schema discovery the connector will query each table or view individually to check access privileges and inaccessible tables, views, or columns therein will be removed. In large schemas, this might cause schema discovery to take too long, in which case it might be advisable to disable this feature.

config_fields.name: checkpoint_target_interval_seconds
config_fields.description: How often (in seconds) a stream should checkpoint, when possible.

config_fields.name: concurrency
config_fields.description: Maximum number of concurrent queries to the database.

config_fields.name: cursor
config_fields.description: Configures how data is extracted from the database.

config_fields.name: database
config_fields.description: The name of the tenant database to connect to. This is required for multi-tenant SAP HANA systems. For single-tenant systems, this can be left empty.

config_fields.name: encryption
config_fields.description: The encryption method with is used when communicating with the database.

config_fields.name: filters
config_fields.description: Inclusion filters for table selection per schema. If no filters are specified for a schema, all tables in that schema will be synced.

config_fields.name: host
config_fields.description: Hostname of the database.

config_fields.name: jdbc_url_params
config_fields.description: Additional properties to pass to the JDBC URL string when connecting to the database formatted as 'key=value' pairs separated by the symbol '&'. (example: key1=value1&key2=value2&key3=value3).

config_fields.name: password
config_fields.description: The password associated with the username.

config_fields.name: port
config_fields.description:
Port of the database.
SAP recommends the following port numbers:
443 - Default listening port for SAP HANA Cloud client connections to the listener.

config_fields.name: schemas
config_fields.description: The list of schemas to sync from. Defaults to user. Case sensitive.

config_fields.name: tunnel_method
config_fields.description: Whether to initiate an SSH tunnel before connecting to the database, and if so, which kind of authentication to use.

config_fields.name: username
config_fields.description: The username which is used to access the database.

check_privileges.description: When this feature is enabled, during schema discovery the connector will query each table or view individually to check access privileges and inaccessible tables, views, or columns therein will be removed. In large schemas, this might cause schema discovery to take too long, in which case it might be advisable to disable this feature.
check_privileges.title: Check Table and Column Access Privileges

cursor.description: Configures how data is extracted from the database.

initial_load_timeout_hours.description: The amount of time an initial load is allowed to continue for before catching up on CDC events.
initial_load_timeout_hours.title: Initial Load Timeout in Hours (Advanced)

invalid_cdc_cursor_position_behavior.description: Determines whether Airbyte should fail or re-sync data in case of an stale/invalid cursor value in the mined logs. If 'Fail sync' is chosen, a user will have to manually reset the connection before being able to continue syncing data. If 'Re-sync data' is chosen, Airbyte will automatically trigger a refresh but could lead to higher cloud costs and data loss.

name: Senseforce

config_fields.name: access_token
config_fields.description: Your API access token. See here. The toke is case sensitive.

config_fields.name: backend_url
config_fields.description: Your Senseforce API backend URL. This is the URL shown during the Login screen. See here for more details. (Note: Most Senseforce backend APIs have the term 'galaxy' in their ULR)

config_fields.name: dataset_id
config_fields.description: The ID of the dataset you want to synchronize. The ID can be found in the URL when opening the dataset. See here for more details. (Note: As the Senseforce API only allows to synchronize a specific dataset, each dataset you want to synchronize needs to be implemented as a separate airbyte source).

config_fields.name: slice_range
config_fields.description: The time increment used by the connector when requesting data from the Senseforce API. The bigger the value is, the less requests will be made and faster the sync will be. On the other hand, the more seldom the state is persisted and the more likely one could run into rate limites.  Furthermore, consider that large chunks of time might take a long time for the Senseforce query to return data - meaning it could take in effect longer than with more smaller time slices. If there are a lot of data per day, set this setting to 1. If there is only very little data per day, you might change the setting to 10 or more.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25. Only data with "Timestamp" after this date will be replicated. Important note: This start date must be set to the first day of where your dataset provides data.  If your dataset has data from 2020-10-10 10:21:10, set the start_date to 2020-10-10 or later

backend_url.title: Senseforce backend URL

dataset_id.description: The ID of the dataset you want to synchronize. The ID can be found in the URL when opening the dataset. See here for more details. (Note: As the Senseforce API only allows to synchronize a specific dataset, each dataset you want to synchronize needs to be implemented as a separate airbyte source).
dataset_id.title: Dataset ID

slice_range.description: The time increment used by the connector when requesting data from the Senseforce API. The bigger the value is, the less requests will be made and faster the sync will be. On the other hand, the more seldom the state is persisted and the more likely one could run into rate limites.  Furthermore, consider that large chunks of time might take a long time for the Senseforce query to return data - meaning it could take in effect longer than with more smaller time slices. If there are a lot of data per day, set this setting to 1. If there is only very little data per day, you might change the setting to 10 or more.
slice_range.title: Data request time increment in days

start_date.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25. Only data with "Timestamp" after this date will be replicated. Important note: This start date must be set to the first day of where your dataset provides data.  If your dataset has data from 2020-10-10 10:21:10, set the start_date to 2020-10-10 or later
start_date.title: The first day (in UTC) when to read data from.

name: Serpstat

config_fields.name: api_key
config_fields.description: Serpstat API key can be found here: https://serpstat.com/users/profile/

config_fields.name: domain
config_fields.description: The domain name to get data for (ex. serpstat.com)

config_fields.name: domains
config_fields.description: The list of domains that will be used in streams that support batch operations

config_fields.name: filter_by
config_fields.description: The field name by which the results should be filtered. Filtering the results will result in fewer API credits spent. Each stream has different filtering options. See https://serpstat.com/api/ for more details.

config_fields.name: filter_value
config_fields.description: The value of the field to filter by. Each stream has different filtering options. See https://serpstat.com/api/ for more details.

config_fields.name: page_size
config_fields.description: The number of data rows per page to be returned. Each data row can contain multiple data points. The max value is 1000. Reducing the size of the page will result in fewer API credits spent.

config_fields.name: pages_to_fetch
config_fields.description: The number of pages that should be fetched. All results will be obtained if left blank. Reducing the number of pages will result in fewer API credits spent.

config_fields.name: region_id
config_fields.description: The ID of a region to get data from in the form of a two-letter country code prepended with the g_ prefix. See the list of supported region IDs here: https://serpstat.com/api/664-request-parameters-v4/.

config_fields.name: sort_by
config_fields.description: The field name by which the results should be sorted. Each stream has different sorting options. See https://serpstat.com/api/ for more details.

config_fields.name: sort_value
config_fields.description: The value of the field to sort by. Each stream has different sorting options. See https://serpstat.com/api/ for more details.

config_fields.description: The server port

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00.000000Z. Any file modified before this date will not be replicated.

config_fields.name: streams
config_fields.description: Each instance of this configuration defines a stream. Use this to define which files belong in the stream, their format, and how they should be parsed and validated. When sending data to warehouse destination such as Snowflake or BigQuery, each stream is a separate table.

config_schema.description:
Used during spec; allows the developer to configure the cloud provider specific options
that are needed when users configure a file-based source.

credentials.description: Credentials for connecting to the SFTP Server

password.description: Password
password.title: Password

credentials.title: Authentication

oneOf.description: Recommended - Extract and load structured records into your destination of choice. This is the classic method of moving data in Airbyte. It allows for blocking and hashing individual fields or files from a structured schema. Data can be flattened, typed and deduped depending on the destination.
oneOf.title: Replicate Records

oneOf.description: Copy raw files without parsing their contents. Bits are copied into the destination exactly as they appeared in the source. Recommended for use with unstructured text data, non-text and compressed files.

preserve_directory_structure.description: If enabled, sends subdirectory folder structure along with source file names to the destination. Otherwise, files will be synced by their names only. This option is ignored when file-based replication is not enabled.
preserve_directory_structure.title: Preserve Sub-Directories in File Paths

oneOf.title: Copy Raw Files

folder_path.description: The directory to search files for sync
folder_path.title: Folder Path

start_date.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00.000000Z. Any file modified before this date will not be replicated.
start_date.title: Start Date

streams.description: Each instance of this configuration defines a stream. Use this to define which files belong in the stream, their format, and how they should be parsed and validated. When sending data to warehouse destination such as Snowflake or BigQuery, each stream is a separate table.

days_to_sync_if_history_is_full.description: When the state history of the file store is full, syncs will only read files that were last modified in the provided day range.
days_to_sync_if_history_is_full.title: Days To Sync If History Is Full

format.description: The configuration options that are used to alter how to read incoming files that deviate from the standard formatting.

double_as_string.description: Whether to convert double fields to strings. This is recommended if you have decimal numbers with a high degree of precision because there can be a loss precision when handling floating point numbers.
double_as_string.title: Convert Double Fields to Strings

delimiter.description: The character delimiting individual cells in the CSV data. This may only be a 1-character string. For tab-delimited data enter '\t'.
delimiter.title: Delimiter

double_quote.description: Whether two quotes in a quoted CSV value denote a single quote in the data.
double_quote.title: Double Quote

encoding.description: The character encoding of the CSV data. Leave blank to default to UTF8. See list of python encodings for allowable options.
encoding.title: Encoding

escape_char.description: The character used for escaping special characters. To disallow escaping, leave this field blank.
escape_char.title: Escape Character

false_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as false values.
false_values.title: False Values

header_definition.description: How headers will be defined. `User Provided` assumes the CSV does not have a header row and uses the headers provided and `Autogenerated` assumes the CSV does not have a header row and the CDK will generate headers using for `f{i}` where `i` is the index starting from 0. Else, the default behavior is to use the header from the CSV file. If a user wants to autogenerate or provide column names for a CSV having headers, they can skip rows.

column_names.description: The column names that will be used while emitting the CSV records
column_names.title: Column Names

header_definition.title: CSV Header Definition

ignore_errors_on_fields_mismatch.description: Whether to ignore errors that occur when the number of fields in the CSV does not match the number of columns in the schema.
ignore_errors_on_fields_mismatch.title: Ignore errors on field mismatch

inference_type.description: How to infer the types of the columns. If none, inference default to strings.
inference_type.title: Inference Type

null_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as null values. For example, if the value 'NA' should be interpreted as null, enter 'NA' in this field.
null_values.title: Null Values

quote_char.description: The character used for quoting CSV values. To disallow quoting, make this field blank.
quote_char.title: Quote Character

skip_rows_after_header.description: The number of rows to skip after the header row.
skip_rows_after_header.title: Skip Rows After Header

skip_rows_before_header.description: The number of rows to skip before the header row. For example, if the header row is on the 3rd row, enter 2 in this field.
skip_rows_before_header.title: Skip Rows Before Header

strings_can_be_null.description: Whether strings can be interpreted as null values. If true, strings that match the null_values set will be interpreted as null. If false, strings that match the null_values set will be interpreted as the string itself.
strings_can_be_null.title: Strings Can Be Null

true_values.description: A set of case-sensitive strings that should be interpreted as true values.
true_values.title: True Values

decimal_as_float.description: Whether to convert decimal fields to floats. There is a loss of precision when converting decimals to floats, so this is not recommended.
decimal_as_float.title: Convert Decimal Fields to Floats

oneOf.description: Extract text from document formats (.pdf, .docx, .md, .pptx) and emit as one record per file.
Process files locally, supporting `fast` and `ocr` modes. This is the default option.
oneOf.title: Local

oneOf.description: Process files via an API, using the `hi_res` mode. This option is useful for increased performance and accuracy, but requires an API key and a hosted instance of unstructured.

api_key.description: The API key to use matching the environment
api_key.title: API Key

api_url.description: The URL of the unstructured API to use
api_url.title: API URL

parameters.description: List of parameters send to the API

name.description: The name of the unstructured API parameter to use
name.title: Parameter name

parameters.title: Additional URL Parameters

oneOf.title: via API

skip_unprocessable_files.description: If true, skip files that cannot be parsed and pass the error message along as the _ab_source_file_parse_error field. If false, fail the sync.
skip_unprocessable_files.title: Skip Unprocessable Files

strategy.description: The strategy used to parse documents. `fast` extracts text directly from the document which doesn't work for all files. `ocr_only` is more reliable, but slower. `hi_res` is the most reliable, but requires an API key and a hosted instance of unstructured and can't be used with local mode. See the unstructured.io documentation for more details: https://unstructured-io.github.io/unstructured/core/partition.html#partition-pdf
strategy.title: Parsing Strategy

oneOf.title: Unstructured Document Format

format.title: Format

globs.description: The pattern used to specify which files should be selected from the file system. For more information on glob pattern matching look here.
globs.title: Globs

input_schema.description: The schema that will be used to validate records extracted from the file. This will override the stream schema that is auto-detected from incoming files.
input_schema.title: Input Schema

legacy_prefix.description: The path prefix configured in v3 versions of the S3 connector. This option is deprecated in favor of a single glob.
legacy_prefix.title: Legacy Prefix

primary_key.description: The column or columns (for a composite key) that serves as the unique identifier of a record. If empty, the primary key will default to the parser's default primary key.
primary_key.title: Primary Key

recent_n_files_to_read_for_schema_discov.description: The number of resent files which will be used to discover the schema for this stream.
recent_n_files_to_read_for_schema_discov.title: Files To Read For Schema Discover

schemaless.description: When enabled, syncs will not validate or structure records against the stream's schema.
schemaless.title: Schemaless

use_first_found_file_for_schema_discover.description: When enabled, the source will use the first found file for schema discovery. Helps to avoid long discovery step.
use_first_found_file_for_schema_discover.title: Use First Found File For Schema Discover

validation_policy.description: The name of the validation policy that dictates sync behavior when a record does not adhere to the stream schema.
validation_policy.title: Validation Policy

streams.title: The list of streams to sync

username.description: The server user
username.title: User Name

config_schema.title: SFTP Bulk Source Spec

name: SharePoint Enterprise

config_fields.name: credentials
config_fields.description: Credentials for connecting to the One Drive API

config_fields.name: file_contains_query
config_fields.description: Input additional query to search files. It will make search files step faster if your Sharepoint account has a lot of files and folders. This query text will be used in the request that will look for files which properties contains inserted text. You can use multiple query texts, they will be applied in search request one by one.

config_fields.name: folder_path
config_fields.description: Path to a specific folder within the drives to search for files. Leave empty to search all folders of the drives. This does not apply to shared items.

config_fields.name: search_scope
config_fields.description: Specifies the location(s) to search for files. Valid options are 'ACCESSIBLE_DRIVES' for all SharePoint drives the user can access, 'SHARED_ITEMS' for shared items the user has access to, and 'ALL' to search both.

config_fields.name: site_url
config_fields.description: Url of SharePoint site to search for files. Leave empty to search in the main site. Use 'https://<tenant_name>.sharepoint.com/sites/' to iterate over all sites.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00.000000Z. Any file modified before this date will not be replicated.

config_fields.name: streams
config_fields.description: Each instance of this configuration defines a stream. Use this to define which files belong in the stream, their format, and how they should be parsed and validated. When sending data to warehouse destination such as Snowflake or BigQuery, each stream is a separate table.

This class uses pydantic for data validation and settings management.

config_fields.name: fulfillment_orders_include_closed
config_fields.description: If enabled, the `Fulfillment Orders` stream includes closed fulfillment orders. Shopify excludes closed orders by default.

config_fields.name: job_checkpoint_interval
config_fields.description: The threshold, after which the single BULK Job should be checkpointed (min: 15k, max: 1M)

config_fields.name: job_product_variants_include_pres_prices
config_fields.description: If enabled, the `Product Variants` stream attempts to include `Presentment prices` field (may affect the performance).

config_fields.name: job_termination_threshold
config_fields.description: The max time in seconds, after which the single BULK Job should be `CANCELED` and retried. The bigger the value the longer the BULK Job is allowed to run.

config_fields.name: lookback_window_in_days
config_fields.description: If set to a positive number, during each incremental sync the connector will re-fetch records from the past N days before the saved state. This helps capture records that may have been missed due to race conditions or late-arriving data. The default value of 0 means no lookback and preserves existing behavior.

name: Slack

config_fields.name: channel_filter
config_fields.description: A channel name list (without leading '#' char) which limit the channels from which you'd like to sync. Empty list means no filter.

config_fields.name: channel_messages_window_size
config_fields.description: The size (in days) of the date window that will be used while syncing data from the channel messages stream. A smaller window will allow for greater parallelization when syncing records, but can lead to rate limiting errors.

config_fields.name: credentials
config_fields.description: Choose how to authenticate into Slack

config_fields.name: include_archived_channels
config_fields.description: Whether to include archived channels in the sync. When disabled (default), archived channels are excluded from the Slack API response, reducing the number of API calls for downstream streams such as channel_messages, threads, and channel_members. Enable this option if you need to sync data from archived channels.

config_fields.name: include_private_channels
config_fields.description: Whether to read information from private channels that the bot is already in.  If false, only public channels will be read.  If true, the bot must be manually added to private channels.

config_fields.name: join_channels
config_fields.description: Whether to join all channels or to sync data only from channels the bot is already in.  If false, you''ll need to manually add the bot to all the channels from which you''d like to sync messages.

config_fields.name: lookback_window
config_fields.description: How far into the past to look for messages in threads, default is 0 days

config_fields.name: num_workers
config_fields.description: The number of worker threads to use for the sync.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00Z. Any data before this date will not be replicated.

config_fields.name: threads_ignore_no_replies
config_fields.description: When enabled, the threads stream will skip messages that have no replies (reply_count is 0, null, or absent), reducing the number of API calls. Disabled by default to make Threads stream contain unthreaded messages in its records.

name: Stripe

config_fields.name: account_id
config_fields.description: Your Stripe account ID (starts with 'acct_', find yours here).

config_fields.name: api_retention_streams
config_fields.description: Select streams where cursor age is validated against the Stripe API 30-day event retention period. When a selected stream's cursor is older than 30 days, the connector performs a full refresh to avoid missing data. Streams not selected here will always use incremental sync regardless of cursor age.

config_fields.name: call_rate_limit
config_fields.description: The number of API calls per second that you allow connector to make. This value can not be bigger than real API call rate limit (https://stripe.com/docs/rate-limits). If not specified the default maximum is 25 calls per second for test/sandbox tokens and 100 for production tokens.

config_fields.name: client_secret
config_fields.description: Stripe API key (usually starts with 'sk_live_'; find yours here).

config_fields.name: lookback_window_days
config_fields.description: When set, the connector will always re-export data from the past N days, where N is the value set here. This is useful if your data is frequently updated after creation. The Lookback Window only applies to streams that do not support event-based incremental syncs: Events, SetupAttempts, ShippingRates, BalanceTransactions, Files, FileLinks, Refunds. More info here

config_fields.name: num_workers
config_fields.description: The number of worker thread to use for the sync. The performance upper boundary depends on call_rate_limit setting and type of account.

config_fields.name: slice_range
config_fields.description: The time increment used by the connector when requesting data from the Stripe API. The bigger the value is, the less requests will be made and faster the sync will be. On the other hand, the more seldom the state is persisted.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: UTC date and time in the format 2017-01-25T00:00:00Z. Only data generated after this date will be replicated.

account_id.description: Your Stripe account ID (starts with 'acct_', find yours here).

config_fields.name: replication_method
config_fields.description: Replication method to use for extracting data from the database. STANDARD replication requires no setup on the DB side but will not be able to represent deletions incrementally. CDC uses the Binlog to detect inserts, updates, and deletes. This needs to be configured on the source database itself.

config_fields.name: ssl
config_fields.description: Encrypt data using SSL. When activating SSL, please select one of the connection modes.

config_fields.name: ssl_mode
config_fields.description: SSL connection modes. disable - Chose this mode to disable encryption of communication between Airbyte and destination database allow - Chose this mode to enable encryption only when required by the destination database prefer - Chose this mode to allow unencrypted connection only if the destination database does not support encryption require - Chose this mode to always require encryption. If the destination database server does not support encryption, connection will fail verify-ca - Chose this mode to always require encryption and to verify that the destination database server has a valid SSL certificate verify-full - This is the most secure mode. Chose this mode to always require encryption and to verify the identity of the destination database server See more information - in the docs.

config_fields.name: end_date
config_fields.description: (Optional) Use this to set the maximum date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the results. Results newer than the end_date will not be shown. Default is set to the current date (today) for incremental syncs.

config_fields.name: query
config_fields.description: (Optional) The query (q) parameter filters the results to only those that include that search term. The q parameter supports AND, OR and NOT operators.

config_fields.name: section
config_fields.description: (Optional) Use this to filter the results by a particular section. See here for a list of all sections, and here for the sections endpoint documentation.

config_fields.name: start_date
config_fields.description: Use this to set the minimum date (YYYY-MM-DD) of the results. Results older than the start_date will not be shown.

config_fields.name: tag
config_fields.description: (Optional) A tag is a piece of data that is used by The Guardian to categorise content. Use this parameter to filter results by showing only the ones matching the entered tag. See here for a list of all tags, and here for the tags endpoint documentation.

config_fields.description: Set to active if you want to include deleted data in report based streams and Ads, Ad Groups and Campaign streams.

config_fields.name: report_granularity
config_fields.description: The number of days per API request for daily report streams. Use the default 30 for most accounts. If syncs fail with TikTok API error 40067 ("query too large"), reduce this value (e.g., 7 or 1). Smaller values make more API requests but avoid query size limits for accounts with many ads.
